---
title: "Bergmann’s Rule across the Pleistocene, Holocene, and Anthropocene in an Eastern Pacific neogastropod, Californiconus californicus (Reeve, 1844) (Gastropoda: Conidae)"
author: "Malloy and Ul-Hasan et al"
date: "Mar 14, 2026"
output:
  pdf_document: default
---

Last edited [Sabah Ul-Hasan]: Mar 14, 2026
https://github.com/MichaelMalloy/C_californicus_Morphology

Contributors of data by order of contribution: MM, SUH, SC, CAO, JKK, WFG
Contributors of code by order of contribution: SUH, LL, MM, SS, CAO

iNat: 
# Include dead or alive?
# Address NA columns (location columns, SST, zooplankton etc)
# Habitat	Depth	BRY	BAR	LMP	TWS	COR	ALG	SCR	BOR_TAP	BOR_STR	BOR_INC



```{r}

##### Data Handling ##### 


# Clean-up of Data-iNat-Raw.csv, ensure path match
iNat <- read.csv("/Users/sulhasan/Downloads/C_californicus_Morphology/data/raw/Data_iNat-Raw.csv")
## Function to check for non-NA, non-empty values
has_values <- function(x) {
  !is.na(x) && x != ""
}
## Initialize empty vectors to store row indices
keep_rows <- c()
remove_rows <- c()
## Iterate through each row of the data frame
for (i in 1:nrow(iNat)) {
  # Check if both length and width have values
  if (has_values(iNat$length[i]) && has_values(iNat$width[i])) {
    keep_rows <- c(keep_rows, i)
  } else {
    remove_rows <- c(remove_rows, i)
  }
}
## Create the subsetted data frame
iNat_keep <- iNat[keep_rows, ]
## Display results
cat(paste("Rows kept:", length(keep_rows), "\n"))
cat(paste("Rows removed:", length(remove_rows), "\n"))
cat(paste("Percentage kept:", round(length(keep_rows)/nrow(iNat)*100, 1), "%\n"))
## Create Globosity_WoverL column and remove length/width columns
iNat_keep$Globosity_WoverL <- iNat_keep$width / iNat_keep$length
iNat_keep <- iNat_keep[, !names(iNat_keep) %in% c("length", "width")]
## Drop specified columns
columns_to_drop <- c("species_name", "observation_license", 
                     "observation_quality", "X")
iNat_keep <- iNat_keep[, !names(iNat_keep) %in% columns_to_drop]
## Rename columns
rename_cols <- c("observation_id" = "Lot_Number", 
                 "observer_login" = "Collector",
                 "observation_latidude" = "Latitude", 
                 "observation_longitude" = "Longitude")
# Only rename columns that exist
for (old_name in names(rename_cols)) {
  if (old_name %in% names(iNat_keep)) {
    names(iNat_keep)[names(iNat_keep) == old_name] <- rename_cols[old_name]
  }
}


# Validation check of Globosity_WoverL between datasets
Raw <- read.csv("/Users/sulhasan/Downloads/C_californicus_Morphology/data/raw/Data_Raw.csv")
## Find common Lot_Numbers between the two datasets
common_lot_numbers <- intersect(iNat_keep$Lot_Number, Raw$Lot_Number)

if (length(common_lot_numbers) > 0) {
  # Create dataframes with matching rows
  iNat_matches <- iNat_keep[iNat_keep$Lot_Number %in% common_lot_numbers, ]
  Raw_matches <- Raw[Raw$Lot_Number %in% common_lot_numbers, ]
  # Merge datasets for comparison
  comparison_df <- merge(
    iNat_matches[, c("Lot_Number", "Globosity_WoverL")],
    Raw_matches[, c("Lot_Number", "Globosity_WoverL")],
    by = "Lot_Number",
    suffixes = c("_iNat", "_Raw")
  )
  # Calculate differences
  comparison_df$Difference <- comparison_df$Globosity_WoverL_iNat - comparison_df$Globosity_WoverL_Raw
  comparison_df$Abs_Difference <- abs(comparison_df$Difference)
  # Summary statistics
  sd_diff <- sd(comparison_df$Difference)
  mean_abs <- mean(comparison_df$Abs_Difference)
  sd_abs <- sd(comparison_df$Abs_Difference)
  cat("\n=== Globosity_WoverL Comparison ===\n")
  cat(paste("Total matching Lot_Numbers:", nrow(comparison_df), "\n"))
  cat(paste("Standard deviation of differences:", round(sd_diff, 6), "\n"))
  # Apply 1 SD threshold
  threshold_1sd <- sd_diff
  rows_to_keep <- comparison_df$Lot_Number[comparison_df$Abs_Difference <= threshold_1sd]
  rows_to_remove <- comparison_df$Lot_Number[comparison_df$Abs_Difference > threshold_1sd]
  cat("\n=== 1 Standard Deviation Filtering ===\n")
  cat(paste("1 SD threshold:", round(threshold_1sd, 6), "\n"))
  cat(paste("Rows within 1 SD:", length(rows_to_keep), 
            "(", round(length(rows_to_keep)/nrow(comparison_df)*100, 1), "%)\n"))
  cat(paste("Rows outside 1 SD:", length(rows_to_remove), 
            "(", round(length(rows_to_remove)/nrow(comparison_df)*100, 1), "%)\n"))
  # Store original row counts before filtering
  iNat_before <- nrow(iNat_keep)
  Raw_before <- nrow(Raw)
  # Filter both datasets to keep only rows within 1 SD
  iNat_keep <- iNat_keep[!iNat_keep$Lot_Number %in% rows_to_remove, ]
  Raw <- Raw[!Raw$Lot_Number %in% rows_to_remove, ]
  cat("\n=== Datasets Updated ===\n")
  cat(paste("iNat_keep: from", iNat_before, "to", nrow(iNat_keep), 
            "rows (removed", iNat_before - nrow(iNat_keep), ")\n"))
  cat(paste("Raw: from", Raw_before, "to", nrow(Raw), 
            "rows (removed", Raw_before - nrow(Raw), ")\n"))
  ## Create comprehensive comparison dataset (moved inside the if statement)
  # Merge full datasets for all matching Lot_Numbers
  full_comparison <- merge(
    iNat_matches[, c("Lot_Number", "observation_date", "Latitude", "Longitude", 
                     "observation_type_0dead", "Globosity_WoverL")],
    Raw_matches[, c("Lot_Number", "Collection", "Ecoregion", "County", "Year", "Month", 
                    "Season", "Globosity_WoverL")],
    by = "Lot_Number",
    suffixes = c("_iNat", "_Raw"),
    all = TRUE
  )
  # Calculate difference and flag if within 1 SD
  full_comparison$Difference <- full_comparison$Globosity_WoverL_iNat - full_comparison$Globosity_WoverL_Raw
  full_comparison$Abs_Difference <- abs(full_comparison$Difference)
  full_comparison$Within_1SD <- full_comparison$Abs_Difference <= sd_diff
  # Add status column
  full_comparison$Status <- ifelse(full_comparison$Within_1SD, "Match", "Mismatch")
  # Sort by absolute difference (largest mismatches first)
  full_comparison <- full_comparison[order(-full_comparison$Abs_Difference), ]
  # Save comprehensive comparison
  write.csv(full_comparison, 
            "/Users/sulhasan/Downloads/C_californicus_Morphology/data/Data_iNat-OverlapValidation.csv", 
            row.names = FALSE)
  cat("\nComprehensive comparison saved as: Data_iNat-OverlapValidation.csv\n")
} else {
  cat("\nNo overlapping Lot_Numbers found between the two datasets.\n")
}


# Merge of Data_iNat-Clean.csv into Data_Raw-Filtered.csv
## Write and save usable data, ensure path
write.csv(iNat_keep, "/Users/sulhasan/Downloads/C_californicus_Morphology/data/Data_iNat-Clean.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(Raw, "/Users/sulhasan/Downloads/C_californicus_Morphology/data/Data_Raw-Filtered.csv", row.names = FALSE)

## Identify Lot_Numbers already in Raw_filtered
existing_lot_numbers <- Raw$Lot_Number
## Split iNat_clean into existing and new records
iNat_existing <- iNat_keep[iNat_keep$Lot_Number %in% existing_lot_numbers, ]
iNat_new <- iNat_keep[!iNat_keep$Lot_Number %in% existing_lot_numbers, ]
cat("\n=== Data Integration ===\n")
cat(paste("Records already in Raw_filtered:", nrow(iNat_existing), "\n"))
cat(paste("New records to add:", nrow(iNat_new), "\n"))
## For new records, create new rows for Raw
if (nrow(iNat_new) > 0) {
  # Step 1: Ensure Latitude and Longitude are numeric
  iNat_new$Latitude <- as.numeric(as.character(iNat_new$Latitude))
  iNat_new$Longitude <- as.numeric(as.character(iNat_new$Longitude))
  # Step 2: Determine starting Specimen_Number
  # Find the maximum existing Specimen_Number
  max_specimen <- max(as.numeric(Raw$Specimen_Number[!is.na(Raw$Specimen_Number)]), na.rm = TRUE)
  if (is.infinite(max_specimen)) max_specimen <- 2996  # Start from 2997 if no existing numbers
  # Step 3: Create a temporary copy of Raw with rounded coordinates for lookup
  Raw_temp <- Raw
  Raw_temp$Latitude <- as.numeric(as.character(Raw_temp$Latitude))
  Raw_temp$Longitude <- as.numeric(as.character(Raw_temp$Longitude))
  # Round coordinates in Raw_temp to 2 decimal places for matching
  Raw_temp$Latitude_rounded <- round(Raw_temp$Latitude, 2)
  Raw_temp$Longitude_rounded <- round(Raw_temp$Longitude, 2)
  # Create lookup tables for location-based matching using rounded coordinates
  location_cols <- c("Latitude_rounded", "Longitude_rounded", "Ecoregion", "County", 
                     "Coastal_Proximity", "Location", "Site", "SoCal_Group", "Island_Size")
  # Get unique location combinations from Raw_temp
  location_lookup <- unique(Raw_temp[, location_cols])
  location_lookup <- location_lookup[complete.cases(location_lookup[, c("Latitude_rounded", "Longitude_rounded")]), ]
  # For SST data - match based on Year, Season, AND Month
  sst_cols <- c("Year", "Season", "Month", "Mean_SST", "Mean_SST_Sdev")
  sst_lookup <- unique(Raw[, sst_cols])
  sst_lookup <- sst_lookup[complete.cases(sst_lookup[, c("Year", "Season", "Month", "Mean_SST")]), ]
  # Step 4: Create lookup for environmental and fisheries columns
  # These columns will be matched by rounded Latitude/Longitude AND Year
  env_cols <- c("Latitude_rounded", "Longitude_rounded", "Year",
                "mean_pvolc3", "sd_pvolc3", 
                "zooplankton_n_points_used", "zooplankton_mean_distance",
                "fish_total_catch_wt_kg", "fish_cpue_kg_per_ha_der",
                "sd_total_catch_wt_kg", "sd_cpue_kg_per_ha_der",
                "fish_n_points_used", "fish_mean_distance",
                "Population_Density", "Population_Density_NoBaja", "MPA")
  # Create lookup table with unique combinations
  env_lookup <- unique(Raw_temp[, env_cols])
  env_lookup <- env_lookup[complete.cases(env_lookup[, c("Latitude_rounded", "Longitude_rounded", "Year")]), ]
  # Create a template dataframe with all Raw columns
  new_rows <- data.frame(matrix(NA, nrow = nrow(iNat_new), ncol = ncol(Raw)))
  colnames(new_rows) <- colnames(Raw)
  # Fill in basic information from iNat_new - but with coordinates rounded to 2 decimals
  new_rows$Specimen_Number <- max_specimen + 1:nrow(iNat_new)
  new_rows$Collection <- "iNat"
  new_rows$Lot_Number <- as.character(iNat_new$Lot_Number)
  new_rows$Collector <- iNat_new$Collector  # Collector already exists in iNat_keep
  new_rows$Latitude <- round(iNat_new$Latitude, 2)  # Round to 2 decimal places
  new_rows$Longitude <- round(iNat_new$Longitude, 2)  # Round to 2 decimal places
  new_rows$Globosity_WoverL <- iNat_new$Globosity_WoverL
  # Extract date components from observation_date
  date_parts <- strsplit(as.character(iNat_new$observation_date), "/")
  new_rows$Month <- as.numeric(sapply(date_parts, function(x) x[1]))
  new_rows$Year <- as.numeric(sapply(date_parts, function(x) {
    year_val <- x[3]
    if (nchar(year_val) == 2) {
      if (as.numeric(year_val) < 50) {
        return(2000 + as.numeric(year_val))
      } else {
        return(1900 + as.numeric(year_val))
      }
    } else {
      return(as.numeric(year_val))
    }
  }))
  # Determine season based on month
  new_rows$Season <- ifelse(new_rows$Month %in% c(12, 1, 2), "Winter",
                          ifelse(new_rows$Month %in% c(3, 4, 5), "Spring",
                          ifelse(new_rows$Month %in% c(6, 7, 8), "Summer", "Fall")))
  # Set Decade based on Year
  new_rows$Decade <- floor(new_rows$Year / 10) * 10
  # Set Epoch (assuming all recent are Anthropocene)
  new_rows$Epoch <- "Anthropocene"
  # Set Formation as NA
  new_rows$Formation <- NA
  # Step 5: Create rounded versions for matching (these will be discarded later)
  new_rows$Latitude_rounded <- new_rows$Latitude  # Already rounded
  new_rows$Longitude_rounded <- new_rows$Longitude  # Already rounded
  # Step 6: Match location-based columns by rounded Latitude/Longitude
  cat("\n=== Matching Location Data ===\n")
  location_match_count <- 0
  for (i in 1:nrow(new_rows)) {
    # Skip if coordinates are NA
    if (is.na(new_rows$Latitude_rounded[i]) || is.na(new_rows$Longitude_rounded[i])) {
      next
    }
    # Find matching rounded coordinates in location_lookup
    coord_match <- which(abs(location_lookup$Latitude_rounded - new_rows$Latitude_rounded[i]) < 0.01 & 
                         abs(location_lookup$Longitude_rounded - new_rows$Longitude_rounded[i]) < 0.01)
    if (length(coord_match) > 0) {
      # Use the first match if multiple
      match_idx <- coord_match[1]
      new_rows$Ecoregion[i] <- location_lookup$Ecoregion[match_idx]
      new_rows$County[i] <- location_lookup$County[match_idx]
      new_rows$Coastal_Proximity[i] <- location_lookup$Coastal_Proximity[match_idx]
      new_rows$Location[i] <- location_lookup$Location[match_idx]
      new_rows$Site[i] <- location_lookup$Site[match_idx]
      new_rows$SoCal_Group[i] <- location_lookup$SoCal_Group[match_idx]
      new_rows$Island_Size[i] <- location_lookup$Island_Size[match_idx]
      location_match_count <- location_match_count + 1
    }
  }
  cat(paste("Location data matched for", location_match_count, "out of", nrow(new_rows), "records\n"))
  # Step 7: Match SST data by Year AND Season AND Month
  cat("\n=== Matching SST Data ===\n")
  sst_match_count <- 0
  for (i in 1:nrow(new_rows)) {
    # Skip if temporal data is missing
    if (is.na(new_rows$Year[i]) || is.na(new_rows$Season[i]) || is.na(new_rows$Month[i])) {
      next
    }
    # Find matching year, season, and month
    year_match <- new_rows$Year[i]
    season_match <- new_rows$Season[i]
    month_match <- new_rows$Month[i]
    # Find exact match for year, season, and month
    match_idx <- which(sst_lookup$Year == year_match & 
                       sst_lookup$Season == season_match & 
                       sst_lookup$Month == month_match)
    
    if (length(match_idx) > 0) {
      # Use the first match if multiple
      idx <- match_idx[1]
      new_rows$Mean_SST[i] <- sst_lookup$Mean_SST[idx]
      new_rows$Mean_SST_Sdev[i] <- sst_lookup$Mean_SST_Sdev[idx]
      sst_match_count <- sst_match_count + 1
    } else {
      # If no exact match, try matching by year and season only (as fallback)
      fallback_idx <- which(sst_lookup$Year == year_match & 
                           sst_lookup$Season == season_match)
      if (length(fallback_idx) > 0) {
        idx <- fallback_idx[1]
        new_rows$Mean_SST[i] <- sst_lookup$Mean_SST[idx]
        new_rows$Mean_SST_Sdev[i] <- sst_lookup$Mean_SST_Sdev[idx]
        sst_match_count <- sst_match_count + 1
      }
    }
  }
  cat(paste("SST data matched for", sst_match_count, "out of", nrow(new_rows), "records\n"))
  # Step 8: Match environmental and fisheries data by rounded coordinates AND Year
  cat("\n=== Matching Environmental & Fisheries Data ===\n")
  env_match_count <- 0
  for (i in 1:nrow(new_rows)) {
    # Skip if coordinates or year are missing
    if (is.na(new_rows$Latitude_rounded[i]) || is.na(new_rows$Longitude_rounded[i]) || is.na(new_rows$Year[i])) {
      next
    }
    # Find matches by rounded coordinates AND year
    env_match <- which(abs(env_lookup$Latitude_rounded - new_rows$Latitude_rounded[i]) < 0.01 & 
                       abs(env_lookup$Longitude_rounded - new_rows$Longitude_rounded[i]) < 0.01 &
                       env_lookup$Year == new_rows$Year[i])
    
    if (length(env_match) > 0) {
      # Use the first match if multiple
      match_idx <- env_match[1]
      # Fill in all environmental and fisheries columns
      new_rows$mean_pvolc3[i] <- env_lookup$mean_pvolc3[match_idx]
      new_rows$sd_pvolc3[i] <- env_lookup$sd_pvolc3[match_idx]
      new_rows$zooplankton_n_points_used[i] <- env_lookup$zooplankton_n_points_used[match_idx]
      new_rows$zooplankton_mean_distance[i] <- env_lookup$zooplankton_mean_distance[match_idx]
      new_rows$fish_total_catch_wt_kg[i] <- env_lookup$fish_total_catch_wt_kg[match_idx]
      new_rows$fish_cpue_kg_per_ha_der[i] <- env_lookup$fish_cpue_kg_per_ha_der[match_idx]
      new_rows$sd_total_catch_wt_kg[i] <- env_lookup$sd_total_catch_wt_kg[match_idx]
      new_rows$sd_cpue_kg_per_ha_der[i] <- env_lookup$sd_cpue_kg_per_ha_der[match_idx]
      new_rows$fish_n_points_used[i] <- env_lookup$fish_n_points_used[match_idx]
      new_rows$fish_mean_distance[i] <- env_lookup$fish_mean_distance[match_idx]
      new_rows$Population_Density[i] <- env_lookup$Population_Density[match_idx]
      new_rows$Population_Density_NoBaja[i] <- env_lookup$Population_Density_NoBaja[match_idx]
      new_rows$MPA[i] <- env_lookup$MPA[match_idx]
      
      env_match_count <- env_match_count + 1
    } else {
      # Fallback: try matching by coordinates only (any year)
      coord_only_match <- which(abs(env_lookup$Latitude_rounded - new_rows$Latitude_rounded[i]) < 0.01 & 
                                abs(env_lookup$Longitude_rounded - new_rows$Longitude_rounded[i]) < 0.01)
      
      if (length(coord_only_match) > 0) {
        match_idx <- coord_only_match[1]
        
        new_rows$mean_pvolc3[i] <- env_lookup$mean_pvolc3[match_idx]
        new_rows$sd_pvolc3[i] <- env_lookup$sd_pvolc3[match_idx]
        new_rows$zooplankton_n_points_used[i] <- env_lookup$zooplankton_n_points_used[match_idx]
        new_rows$zooplankton_mean_distance[i] <- env_lookup$zooplankton_mean_distance[match_idx]
        new_rows$fish_total_catch_wt_kg[i] <- env_lookup$fish_total_catch_wt_kg[match_idx]
        new_rows$fish_cpue_kg_per_ha_der[i] <- env_lookup$fish_cpue_kg_per_ha_der[match_idx]
        new_rows$sd_total_catch_wt_kg[i] <- env_lookup$sd_total_catch_wt_kg[match_idx]
        new_rows$sd_cpue_kg_per_ha_der[i] <- env_lookup$sd_cpue_kg_per_ha_der[match_idx]
        new_rows$fish_n_points_used[i] <- env_lookup$fish_n_points_used[match_idx]
        new_rows$fish_mean_distance[i] <- env_lookup$fish_mean_distance[match_idx]
        new_rows$Population_Density[i] <- env_lookup$Population_Density[match_idx]
        new_rows$Population_Density_NoBaja[i] <- env_lookup$Population_Density_NoBaja[match_idx]
        new_rows$MPA[i] <- env_lookup$MPA[match_idx]
        
        env_match_count <- env_match_count + 1
      }
    }
  }
  
  cat(paste("Environmental/fisheries data matched for", env_match_count, "out of", nrow(new_rows), "records\n"))
  # Remove temporary rounded coordinate columns before appending
  new_rows$Latitude_rounded <- NULL
  new_rows$Longitude_rounded <- NULL
  # Append new rows to Raw
  Raw <- rbind(Raw, new_rows)
  cat("\n=== New Records Added ===\n")
  cat(paste("Assigned Specimen_Numbers:", min(new_rows$Specimen_Number), "to", max(new_rows$Specimen_Number), "\n"))
}
## Round all coordinates in the entire Raw dataset to 2 decimal places for consistency
Raw$Latitude <- round(as.numeric(as.character(Raw$Latitude)), 2)
Raw$Longitude <- round(as.numeric(as.character(Raw$Longitude)), 2)
## Sort by Specimen_Number (smallest to largest)
Raw$Specimen_Number <- as.numeric(as.character(Raw$Specimen_Number))
Raw <- Raw[order(Raw$Specimen_Number), ]
## Save the updated dataset
write.csv(Raw, 
          "/Users/sulhasan/Downloads/C_californicus_Morphology/data/Data_Raw-Integrated.csv", 
          row.names = FALSE)
cat(paste("Final integrated dataset has", nrow(Raw), "rows\n"))
cat("Saved as: Data_Raw-Integrated.csv\n")


##### Data Handling ##### 
```

later

Data Clean-up       Post-processing: Raw -> Clean -> SoCal      Lines 25 - 154
Fig 1a-b            Map of C. californicus range                Lines 156 - 272
Fig 2b              Shell allometry (shell manual)              Lines 274 - 329

Fig 4a-f,Supp Fig 2 Size or Shape and Ecoregion + Proxmity      Lines 475 - 630
Tables 1-2          Statistics breakdown (of all figures)       Lines 632 - 702
Supp Fig 3          Correlations to human population density    Lines 704 - 769
Cope's Rule         Size by location ~ Epoch                    Lines 771 - 

- Make sure code below reflects figures and tables (work backwards from figures/tables in folder to code to formalize)
- Update map to include iNat raw for natural history map (make sure no duplicates)
- Compare in other models, and ask this one what prompt it would give to start from scratch
- Need to bin islands by proximity to coast and each other
- Why is Socal and Clean the same as Raw for rows?


```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# COMPLETE ANALYSIS PIPELINE: Californiconus californicus Morphology
# Testing Bergmann's Rule across Pleistocene, Holocene, and Anthropocene
# ==============================================================================

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 1: PACKAGE INSTALLATION AND LOADING
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("SECTION 1: INSTALLING AND LOADING PACKAGES\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# Required packages
required_packages <- c(
  # Data manipulation
  "tidyverse", "lubridate", "broom",
  
  # Statistical tests
  "nortest", "moments", "car", "lmtest", "boot", "pwr", "effectsize",
  
  # Regression models
  "MASS", "lme4", "nlme", "mgcv", "emmeans",
  
  # Spatial and maps
  "sf", "ggspatial", "maps", "mapdata",
  
  # Visualization
  "ggplot2", "cowplot", "ggsignif", "ggpubr", "gridExtra", "GGally",
  
  # Web data
  "RCurl"
)

# Function to safely install missing packages
install_if_missing <- function(packages) {
  installed <- rownames(installed.packages())
  to_install <- packages[!packages %in% installed]
  
  if(length(to_install) > 0) {
    cat("Installing missing packages:", paste(to_install, collapse = ", "), "\n")
    install.packages(to_install, dependencies = TRUE, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
  } else {
    cat("All required packages are already installed.\n")
  }
}

# Install missing packages
install_if_missing(required_packages)

# Load all packages
cat("\nLoading packages...\n")
for(pkg in required_packages) {
  library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
}
cat("✓ All packages loaded successfully\n")

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 2: CREATE OUTPUT DIRECTORIES
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("SECTION 2: CREATING OUTPUT DIRECTORIES\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# Create main directories
dirs <- c("data", "data/null", "results", "results/figures", "results/summary")
for(d in dirs) {
  if(!dir.exists(d)) {
    dir.create(d, recursive = TRUE)
    cat("✓ Created directory:", d, "\n")
  } else {
    cat("✓ Directory exists:", d, "\n")
  }
}

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 3: DATA LOADING AND CLEANING
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("SECTION 3: DATA LOADING AND CLEANING\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# Function to load data from GitHub
load_data_from_github <- function() {
  
  cat("Loading data from GitHub repository...\n")
  
  # Define GitHub base URL
  base_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sabahzero/C_californicus_Morphology/main/"
  
  # Files to load
  files <- list(
    raw = "data/Data-Raw.csv",
    key = "data/Data-Key.csv"
  )
  
  # Function to load a single file
  try_load <- function(filename, description) {
    url <- paste0(base_url, filename)
    tryCatch({
      data <- read.csv(text = getURL(url), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
      cat("  ✓ Loaded", description, "(", nrow(data), "rows )\n")
      return(data)
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("  ✗ Failed to load", description, ":", e$message, "\n")
      return(NULL)
    })
  }
  
  # Load files
  data_list <- list()
  data_list$raw <- try_load(files$raw, "raw data")
  data_list$key <- try_load(files$key, "data key")
  
  if(is.null(data_list$raw)) {
    stop("Cannot proceed without raw data file")
  }
  
  return(data_list)
}

# Function to clean data with detailed diagnostics
clean_raw_data <- function(raw_data, key_data) {
  
  cat("\nCleaning raw data...\n")
  
  # Make a copy
  data <- raw_data
  
  # Initial row count
  initial_rows <- nrow(data)
  cat("  Initial rows:", initial_rows, "\n")
  
  # Store raw data for comparison
  raw_data_saved <- data
  
  # Step 1: Check for Pliocene specimens
  if("Epoch" %in% names(data)) {
    cat("  Epoch distribution in raw data:\n")
    print(table(data$Epoch, useNA = "ifany"))
    
    data <- data[data$Epoch != "Pliocene", ]
    cat("  After removing Pliocene:", nrow(data), "rows (removed", initial_rows - nrow(data), ")\n")
  }
  
  # Step 2: Filter latitude range
  if("Latitude" %in% names(data)) {
    before_lat <- nrow(data)
    data <- data[data$Latitude > 26.43 & data$Latitude < 38.16 & !is.na(data$Latitude), ]
    cat("  After latitude filtering:", nrow(data), "rows (removed", before_lat - nrow(data), ")\n")
  }
  
  # Step 3: Filter size outliers
  if("Length_mm" %in% names(data)) {
    before_len <- nrow(data)
    data <- data[data$Length_mm >= 5 & data$Length_mm <= 50 & !is.na(data$Length_mm), ]
    cat("  After length filtering (5-50mm):", nrow(data), "rows (removed", before_len - nrow(data), ")\n")
  }
  
  if("Width_mm" %in% names(data)) {
    before_wid <- nrow(data)
    data <- data[data$Width_mm >= 2 & data$Width_mm <= 30 & !is.na(data$Width_mm), ]
    cat("  After width filtering (2-30mm):", nrow(data), "rows (removed", before_wid - nrow(data), ")\n")
  }
  
  # Step 4: Calculate derived metrics
  if(all(c("Length_mm", "Width_mm") %in% names(data))) {
    data$Geometric_Mean <- sqrt(data$Length_mm * data$Width_mm)
    data$Globosity_WoverL <- data$Width_mm / data$Length_mm
    
    # Filter globosity
    before_glob <- nrow(data)
    data <- data[data$Globosity_WoverL >= 0.3 & data$Globosity_WoverL <= 0.8 & !is.na(data$Globosity_WoverL), ]
    cat("  After globosity filtering:", nrow(data), "rows (removed", before_glob - nrow(data), ")\n")
  }
  
  # Step 5: Group by lot
  if("Lot_Number" %in% names(data)) {
    before_lot <- nrow(data)
    # Calculate specimens per lot
    lot_counts <- table(data$Lot_Number)
    lots_to_keep <- names(lot_counts[lot_counts > 2])
    
    # Keep lots with >2 specimens OR iNaturalist
    data <- data[data$Lot_Number %in% lots_to_keep | data$Collection == "iNat" | is.na(data$Collection), ]
    cat("  After lot filtering:", nrow(data), "rows (removed", before_lot - nrow(data), ")\n")
  }
  
  # Step 6: Create factor variables
  if("Epoch" %in% names(data)) {
    data$Epoch <- factor(data$Epoch, levels = c("Pleistocene", "Holocene", "Anthropocene"))
  }
  
  if("Collection" %in% names(data)) {
    data$Data_Type <- ifelse(data$Collection == "iNat", "iNaturalist", "Museum/Field")
    data$Data_Type <- factor(data$Data_Type)
  }
  
  if("Ecoregion" %in% names(data)) {
    data$Ecoregion <- factor(data$Ecoregion)
  }
  
  if("Coastal_Proximity" %in% names(data)) {
    data$Coastal_Proximity <- factor(data$Coastal_Proximity)
  }
  
  cat("\n  Cleaning summary:\n")
  cat("    Initial rows:", initial_rows, "\n")
  cat("    Final rows:", nrow(data), "\n")
  cat("    Rows removed:", initial_rows - nrow(data), 
      sprintf("(%.1f%%)", (initial_rows - nrow(data))/initial_rows * 100), "\n")
  
  # Return both raw and cleaned for comparison
  return(list(cleaned = data, raw = raw_data_saved))
}

# Function to create SoCal subset
create_socal_subset <- function(data) {
  
  cat("\nCreating Southern California subset...\n")
  
  socal_counties <- c("Santa Barbara", "Ventura", "Los Angeles", "Orange", "San Diego")
  
  if("County" %in% names(data)) {
    SoCal <- data[data$County %in% socal_counties, ]
    
    cat("  SoCal subset created:\n")
    cat("    Rows:", nrow(SoCal), "\n")
    cat("    Percentage of full dataset:", 
        sprintf("%.1f%%", nrow(SoCal)/nrow(data) * 100), "\n")
    
    return(SoCal)
  } else {
    cat("  Warning: County column not found. Cannot create SoCal subset.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
}

# Main data loading and cleaning
cat("\n--- Starting data loading pipeline ---\n")

# Load data
data_list <- load_data_from_github()

# Clean data
clean_results <- clean_raw_data(data_list$raw, data_list$key)
Clean <- clean_results$cleaned
Raw <- clean_results$raw

# Save raw data for reference
write.csv(Raw, "data/Data-Raw-Reference.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n✓ Saved raw reference dataset to: data/Data-Raw-Reference.csv\n")

# Save cleaned data
write.csv(Clean, "data/Data_Clean.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Saved Clean dataset to: data/Data_Clean.csv\n")

# Create SoCal subset
SoCal <- create_socal_subset(Clean)

if(!is.null(SoCal)) {
  write.csv(SoCal, "data/Data_SoCal.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("✓ Saved SoCal subset to: data/Data_SoCal.csv\n")
}

# Display final summary
cat("\n=== DATASET SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("Raw dataset dimensions:", nrow(Raw), "rows,", ncol(Raw), "columns\n")
cat("Clean dataset dimensions:", nrow(Clean), "rows,", ncol(Clean), "columns\n")
if(!is.null(SoCal)) cat("SoCal dataset dimensions:", nrow(SoCal), "rows,", ncol(SoCal), "columns\n")

if("Epoch" %in% names(Clean)) {
  cat("\nSpecimens by epoch (Clean):\n")
  print(table(Clean$Epoch))
}

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 4: MAP VISUALIZATIONS
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("SECTION 4: CREATING DISTRIBUTION MAPS\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# Function to create distribution maps
create_distribution_maps <- function(raw_data, clean_data, socal_data) {
  
  cat("Creating distribution maps...\n")
  
  # Get base map data for broader region (includes Mexico)
  west_coast_map <- map_data("world", region = c("USA", "Mexico"))
  california_map <- map_data("state", region = "california")
  baja_map <- map_data("world", region = "Mexico")
  
  # Get map bounds for full distribution
  all_lats <- c(raw_data$Latitude, clean_data$Latitude)
  all_lons <- c(raw_data$Longitude, clean_data$Longitude)
  
  lat_range <- range(all_lats, na.rm = TRUE) + c(-2, 2)
  lon_range <- range(all_lons, na.rm = TRUE) + c(-2, 2)
  
  # Map 1: Raw data distribution
  cat("  Creating raw data distribution map...\n")
  
  p1 <- ggplot() +
    # Base map
    geom_polygon(data = west_coast_map, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group),
                 fill = "lightgray", color = "white", size = 0.2) +
    # Points colored by epoch
    geom_point(data = raw_data, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, color = Epoch),
               alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +
    # Formatting
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Pleistocene" = "#00AFBB", 
                                   "Holocene" = "#E7B800", 
                                   "Anthropocene" = "#FC4E07",
                                   "Pliocene" = "#993404")) +
    coord_fixed(xlim = lon_range, ylim = lat_range, ratio = 1.3) +
    labs(title = "Raw Data: All Sampling Locations",
         subtitle = paste("n =", nrow(raw_data), "specimens"),
         x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude",
         color = "Epoch") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
  
  ggsave("results/figures/Map1_Raw_Distribution.png", p1, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
  
  # Map 2: Clean data distribution
  cat("  Creating clean data distribution map...\n")
  
  p2 <- ggplot() +
    geom_polygon(data = west_coast_map, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group),
                 fill = "lightgray", color = "white", size = 0.2) +
    geom_point(data = clean_data, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, color = Epoch),
               alpha = 0.6, size = 1.2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Pleistocene" = "#00AFBB", 
                                   "Holocene" = "#E7B800", 
                                   "Anthropocene" = "#FC4E07")) +
    coord_fixed(xlim = lon_range, ylim = lat_range, ratio = 1.3) +
    labs(title = "Clean Data: Sampling Locations After Filtering",
         subtitle = paste("n =", nrow(clean_data), "specimens"),
         x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude",
         color = "Epoch") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
  
  ggsave("results/figures/Map2_Clean_Distribution.png", p2, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
  
  # Map 3: SoCal zoom if data available
  if(!is.null(socal_data) && nrow(socal_data) > 0) {
    cat("  Creating Southern California zoom map...\n")
    
    # SoCal bounds
    socal_lat_range <- range(socal_data$Latitude, na.rm = TRUE) + c(-0.3, 0.3)
    socal_lon_range <- range(socal_data$Longitude, na.rm = TRUE) + c(-0.3, 0.3)
    
    # Add density contours
    p3 <- ggplot() +
      # Base map
      geom_polygon(data = west_coast_map, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group),
                   fill = "lightgray", color = "darkgray", size = 0.2) +
      # Density contours
      stat_density2d(data = socal_data, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, fill = after_stat(density)),
                     geom = "tile", contour = FALSE, alpha = 0.5) +
      scale_fill_gradient(low = "yellow", high = "red", name = "Density") +
      # Points
      geom_point(data = socal_data, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, color = Epoch),
                 alpha = 0.7, size = 1.5) +
      # Channel Islands label
      annotate("text", x = -119.8, y = 34.0, label = "Channel Islands", 
               size = 3, fontface = "italic") +
      # Formatting
      coord_fixed(xlim = socal_lon_range, ylim = socal_lat_range, ratio = 1.3) +
      scale_color_manual(values = c("Pleistocene" = "#00AFBB", 
                                     "Holocene" = "#E7B800", 
                                     "Anthropocene" = "#FC4E07")) +
      labs(title = "Southern California: Zoomed View with Density",
           subtitle = paste("n =", nrow(socal_data), "specimens"),
           x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude",
           color = "Epoch") +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom",
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
            plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
    
    ggsave("results/figures/Map3_SoCal_Zoom_Density.png", p3, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
  }
  
  cat("✓ All maps saved to results/figures/\n")
}

# Create maps
create_distribution_maps(Raw, Clean, SoCal)

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 5: PRE-ANALYSIS DIAGNOSTICS
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("SECTION 5: PRE-ANALYSIS DIAGNOSTICS\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# Function for distribution assessment
assess_distribution <- function(data, variable, group_var = NULL) {
  
  cat(paste0("  Assessing ", variable, "...\n"))
  
  # Extract values and ensure they're numeric
  values <- data[[variable]]
  
  # Convert to numeric if needed
  if(!is.numeric(values)) {
    values <- as.numeric(as.character(values))
  }
  
  # Remove NAs
  values <- values[!is.na(values) & is.finite(values)]
  
  if(length(values) == 0) {
    return(list(n = 0, mean = NA, sd = NA, skewness = NA, kurtosis = NA, 
                ad_p = NA, is_normal = FALSE, values = numeric(0)))
  }
  
  # Statistical tests
  ad_test <- tryCatch(ad.test(values), error = function(e) list(p.value = NA))
  
  # Skewness and kurtosis
  skew_val <- tryCatch(skewness(values), error = function(e) NA)
  kurt_val <- tryCatch(kurtosis(values), error = function(e) NA)
  
  return(list(
    variable = variable,
    n = length(values),
    mean = mean(values),
    median = median(values),
    sd = sd(values),
    skewness = skew_val,
    kurtosis = kurt_val,
    ad_p = ifelse(is.null(ad_test$p.value), NA, ad_test$p.value),
    is_normal = ifelse(!is.na(ad_test$p.value), ad_test$p.value > 0.05, FALSE),
    values = values
  ))
}

# Run distribution assessments
cat("Assessing variable distributions...\n")

dist_length <- assess_distribution(Clean, "Length_mm", "Epoch")
dist_width <- assess_distribution(Clean, "Width_mm", "Epoch")
dist_glob <- assess_distribution(Clean, "Globosity_WoverL", "Epoch")

# Power analysis
cat("\nPerforming power analysis...\n")

n_total <- nrow(Clean)
if(n_total > 0) {
  power_cor_small <- tryCatch(pwr.r.test(n = n_total, r = 0.1, sig.level = 0.05)$power, error = function(e) NA)
  power_cor_medium <- tryCatch(pwr.r.test(n = n_total, r = 0.3, sig.level = 0.05)$power, error = function(e) NA)
  min_detectable_r <- tryCatch(pwr.r.test(n = n_total, power = 0.80, sig.level = 0.05)$r, error = function(e) NA)
  
  # Save power results
  sink("results/summary/Power_Analysis_Results.txt")
  cat("POWER ANALYSIS RESULTS\n")
  cat("======================\n\n")
  cat("Total sample size:", n_total, "\n\n")
  cat("Power to detect correlations:\n")
  cat("  Small effect (r = 0.1):", ifelse(!is.na(power_cor_small), 
                                          paste0(round(power_cor_small * 100, 1), "%"), "N/A"), "\n")
  cat("  Medium effect (r = 0.3):", ifelse(!is.na(power_cor_medium), 
                                           paste0(round(power_cor_medium * 100, 1), "%"), "N/A"), "\n")
  cat("  Minimum detectable r with 80% power:", 
      ifelse(!is.na(min_detectable_r), round(min_detectable_r, 3), "N/A"), "\n\n")
  
  cat("RECOMMENDATIONS:\n")
  if(!is.null(dist_length) && !dist_length$is_normal) {
    cat("  - Use non-parametric tests (Spearman correlation)\n")
    cat("  - Consider robust regression (MASS::rlm)\n")
  }
  sink()
}

# Create null datasets
cat("\nCreating null datasets for validation...\n")

set.seed(12345)
n_perm <- 1000

# 1. Complete randomization
null_complete <- Clean
null_complete$Length_mm <- sample(null_complete$Length_mm)

# 2. Epoch-stratified randomization
null_epoch <- Clean
if("Epoch" %in% names(Clean)) {
  for(epoch in unique(Clean$Epoch)) {
    idx <- which(Clean$Epoch == epoch)
    if(length(idx) > 1) {
      null_epoch$Length_mm[idx] <- sample(Clean$Length_mm[idx])
    }
  }
}

# 3. Latitude-stratified
null_stratified <- Clean
lat_bins <- cut(Clean$Latitude, breaks = 10)
for(bin in levels(lat_bins)) {
  idx <- which(lat_bins == bin)
  if(length(idx) > 1) {
    null_stratified$Length_mm[idx] <- sample(Clean$Length_mm[idx])
  }
}

# 4. Bootstrap
boot_idx <- sample(1:nrow(Clean), size = nrow(Clean), replace = TRUE)
null_bootstrap <- Clean[boot_idx, ]

# 5. Permutation distribution
perm_correlations <- numeric(n_perm)
for(i in 1:n_perm) {
  perm_data <- Clean
  perm_data$Length_mm <- sample(perm_data$Length_mm)
  perm_correlations[i] <- tryCatch({
    cor(perm_data$Length_mm, perm_data$Latitude, 
        method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
  }, error = function(e) NA)
}

# Save null datasets to data/null/
write.csv(null_complete, "data/null/NullDataset_Complete.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(null_epoch, "data/null/NullDataset_Epoch.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(null_stratified, "data/null/NullDataset_Stratified.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(null_bootstrap, "data/null/NullDataset_Bootstrap.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(data.frame(perm_correlation = perm_correlations), 
          "data/null/Permutation_Distribution.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Null datasets saved to data/null/\n")

# Create distribution summary table
dist_summary <- data.frame(
  Variable = c("Length (mm)", "Width (mm)", "Globosity (W/L)"),
  N = c(dist_length$n, dist_width$n, dist_glob$n),
  Mean = round(c(dist_length$mean, dist_width$mean, dist_glob$mean), 2),
  Median = round(c(dist_length$median, dist_width$median, dist_glob$median), 2),
  SD = round(c(dist_length$sd, dist_width$sd, dist_glob$sd), 2),
  Skewness = round(c(dist_length$skewness, dist_width$skewness, dist_glob$skewness), 3),
  Kurtosis = round(c(dist_length$kurtosis, dist_width$kurtosis, dist_glob$kurtosis), 3),
  AD_p = round(c(dist_length$ad_p, dist_width$ad_p, dist_glob$ad_p), 4),
  Normal = c(dist_length$is_normal, dist_width$is_normal, dist_glob$is_normal)
)

write.csv(dist_summary, "results/TableS1_Distribution_Summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n✓ Distribution summary saved to results/TableS1_Distribution_Summary.csv\n")

# Create pre-analysis summary
sink("results/summary/PreAnalysis_Summary.txt")
cat("PRE-ANALYSIS DATA QUALITY REPORT\n")
cat("================================\n\n")
cat("DATA SUMMARY:\n")
cat("  Total specimens (Clean):", nrow(Clean), "\n")
cat("  By epoch:", paste(names(table(Clean$Epoch)), table(Clean$Epoch), sep = "=", collapse = ", "), "\n\n")
cat("NORMALITY ASSESSMENT:\n")
cat("  Length: p =", format.pval(dist_length$ad_p, digits = 3), 
    ifelse(dist_length$is_normal, " (NORMAL)", " (NON-NORMAL)"), "\n")
cat("  Width: p =", format.pval(dist_width$ad_p, digits = 3),
    ifelse(dist_width$is_normal, " (NORMAL)", " (NON-NORMAL)"), "\n")
cat("  Globosity: p =", format.pval(dist_glob$ad_p, digits = 3),
    ifelse(dist_glob$is_normal, " (NORMAL)", " (NON-NORMAL)"), "\n\n")
cat("RECOMMENDATIONS:\n")
if(!dist_length$is_normal) {
  cat("  - Use Spearman correlation (non-parametric)\n")
  cat("  - Report effect sizes with confidence intervals\n")
}
sink()

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 6: MAIN ANALYSIS - RESEARCH QUESTIONS
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("SECTION 6: MAIN ANALYSIS - RESEARCH QUESTIONS\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# Initialize results dataframes
bergmann_results <- data.frame()
shape_results <- data.frame()

# Q1: Overall Bergmann's Rule
cat("\n--- Q1: Testing Bergmann's Rule (Length ~ Latitude) ---\n")

spearman_all <- tryCatch({
  cor.test(Clean$Length_mm, Clean$Latitude, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
}, error = function(e) NULL)

if(!is.null(spearman_all)) {
  # Bootstrap CI
  set.seed(12345)
  boot_cors <- numeric(1000)
  for(i in 1:1000) {
    boot_idx <- sample(1:nrow(Clean), size = nrow(Clean), replace = TRUE)
    boot_data <- Clean[boot_idx, ]
    boot_cors[i] <- cor(boot_data$Length_mm, boot_data$Latitude, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
  }
  ci_lower <- quantile(boot_cors, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE)
  ci_upper <- quantile(boot_cors, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  bergmann_results <- rbind(bergmann_results, data.frame(
    Model = "Length ~ Latitude (Overall)",
    N = nrow(Clean),
    Slope = NA,  # Spearman doesn't give slope
    Rho = round(spearman_all$estimate, 4),
    CI_lower = round(ci_lower, 4),
    CI_upper = round(ci_upper, 4),
    P_value = format.pval(spearman_all$p.value, digits = 3),
    Supports_Bergmann = ifelse(spearman_all$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
  ))
}

# Q1a: By epoch
cat("\n--- Q1a: By Epoch ---\n")

if("Epoch" %in% names(Clean)) {
  for(epoch in levels(Clean$Epoch)) {
    epoch_data <- Clean[Clean$Epoch == epoch, ]
    if(nrow(epoch_data) > 5) {
      test <- tryCatch({
        cor.test(epoch_data$Length_mm, epoch_data$Latitude, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
      }, error = function(e) NULL)
      
      if(!is.null(test)) {
        bergmann_results <- rbind(bergmann_results, data.frame(
          Model = paste("Length ~ Latitude (", epoch, ")", sep = ""),
          N = nrow(epoch_data),
          Slope = NA,
          Rho = round(test$estimate, 4),
          CI_lower = NA,
          CI_upper = NA,
          P_value = format.pval(test$p.value, digits = 3),
          Supports_Bergmann = ifelse(test$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
        ))
        
        cat("  ", epoch, ": ρ =", round(test$estimate, 4), 
            ", p =", format.pval(test$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
      }
    }
  }
}

# Test for interaction using linear model
if("Epoch" %in% names(Clean) && length(unique(Clean$Epoch)) > 1) {
  lm_interaction <- lm(Length_mm ~ Latitude * Epoch, data = Clean)
  lm_main <- lm(Length_mm ~ Latitude + Epoch, data = Clean)
  anova_result <- anova(lm_main, lm_interaction)
  
  bergmann_results <- rbind(bergmann_results, data.frame(
    Model = "Length ~ Latitude * Epoch (Interaction)",
    N = nrow(Clean),
    Slope = NA,
    Rho = NA,
    CI_lower = NA,
    CI_upper = NA,
    P_value = format.pval(anova_result$`Pr(>F)`[2], digits = 3),
    Supports_Bergmann = ifelse(anova_result$`Pr(>F)`[2] < 0.05, "Varies", "Consistent")
  ))
}

# Q1b: SoCal mainland vs island
cat("\n--- Q1b: SoCal Mainland vs Island ---\n")

if(exists("SoCal") && !is.null(SoCal) && "Coastal_Proximity" %in% names(SoCal)) {
  for(prox in levels(SoCal$Coastal_Proximity)) {
    prox_data <- SoCal[SoCal$Coastal_Proximity == prox, ]
    if(nrow(prox_data) > 5) {
      test <- tryCatch({
        cor.test(prox_data$Length_mm, prox_data$Latitude, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
      }, error = function(e) NULL)
      
      if(!is.null(test)) {
        bergmann_results <- rbind(bergmann_results, data.frame(
          Model = paste("SoCal -", prox),
          N = nrow(prox_data),
          Slope = NA,
          Rho = round(test$estimate, 4),
          CI_lower = NA,
          CI_upper = NA,
          P_value = format.pval(test$p.value, digits = 3),
          Supports_Bergmann = ifelse(test$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
        ))
        
        cat("  ", prox, ": ρ =", round(test$estimate, 4), 
            ", p =", format.pval(test$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
      }
    }
  }
}

# Q2: Shell Shape
cat("\n--- Q2: Shell Shape (Globosity ~ Latitude) ---\n")

spearman_shape <- tryCatch({
  cor.test(Clean$Globosity_WoverL, Clean$Latitude, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
}, error = function(e) NULL)

if(!is.null(spearman_shape)) {
  shape_results <- rbind(shape_results, data.frame(
    Model = "Globosity ~ Latitude (Overall)",
    N = nrow(Clean),
    Rho = round(spearman_shape$estimate, 4),
    P_value = format.pval(spearman_shape$p.value, digits = 3),
    Significant = ifelse(spearman_shape$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
  ))
}

# Q2a: Shape by epoch
cat("\n--- Q2a: Shape by Epoch ---\n")

if("Epoch" %in% names(Clean)) {
  for(epoch in levels(Clean$Epoch)) {
    epoch_data <- Clean[Clean$Epoch == epoch, ]
    if(nrow(epoch_data) > 5) {
      test <- tryCatch({
        cor.test(epoch_data$Globosity_WoverL, epoch_data$Latitude, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
      }, error = function(e) NULL)
      
      if(!is.null(test)) {
        shape_results <- rbind(shape_results, data.frame(
          Model = paste("Globosity ~ Latitude (", epoch, ")", sep = ""),
          N = nrow(epoch_data),
          Rho = round(test$estimate, 4),
          P_value = format.pval(test$p.value, digits = 3),
          Significant = ifelse(test$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
        ))
        
        cat("  ", epoch, ": ρ =", round(test$estimate, 4), 
            ", p =", format.pval(test$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
      }
    }
  }
}

# Save results tables
write.csv(bergmann_results, "results/Table1_Bergmanns_Rule.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(shape_results, "results/Table2_Shape_Results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n✓ Results tables saved to results/\n")

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 7: VISUALIZATIONS
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("SECTION 7: CREATING VISUALIZATIONS\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# Define colors
epoch_colors <- c("Pleistocene" = "#00AFBB", 
                  "Holocene" = "#E7B800", 
                  "Anthropocene" = "#FC4E07")

# Figure 1: Bergmann's Rule
p1 <- ggplot(Clean, aes(x = Latitude, y = Length_mm, color = Epoch)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, aes(fill = Epoch), alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
  labs(title = "Bergmann's Rule: Shell Length vs Latitude",
       subtitle = paste("n =", nrow(Clean), "specimens"),
       x = "Latitude (°N)", y = "Shell Length (mm)",
       color = "Epoch", fill = "Epoch") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave("results/figures/Figure1_Bergmanns_Rule.png", p1, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Figure 2: Shell Shape
p2 <- ggplot(Clean, aes(x = Latitude, y = Globosity_WoverL, color = Epoch)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, aes(fill = Epoch), alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
  labs(title = "Shell Shape (Globosity) vs Latitude",
       subtitle = paste("n =", nrow(Clean), "specimens"),
       x = "Latitude (°N)", y = "Globosity (W/L ratio)",
       color = "Epoch", fill = "Epoch") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave("results/figures/Figure2_Shell_Shape.png", p2, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Figure 3: Boxplots by epoch
p3a <- ggplot(Clean, aes(x = Epoch, y = Length_mm, fill = Epoch)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
  labs(title = "Shell Length by Epoch", x = "Epoch", y = "Length (mm)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p3b <- ggplot(Clean, aes(x = Epoch, y = Globosity_WoverL, fill = Epoch)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
  labs(title = "Shell Shape by Epoch", x = "Epoch", y = "Globosity (W/L)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p3 <- plot_grid(p3a, p3b, ncol = 2, labels = "AUTO")
ggsave("results/figures/Figure3_Epoch_Boxplots.png", p3, width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# Figure 4: Validation against null
if(length(perm_correlations) > 0) {
  perm_cors <- perm_correlations[!is.na(perm_correlations)]
  if(length(perm_cors) > 0) {
    empirical_p <- mean(abs(perm_cors) >= abs(spearman_all$estimate), na.rm = TRUE)
    
    p4 <- ggplot(data.frame(perm = perm_cors), aes(x = perm)) +
      geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "lightgray", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
      geom_vline(xintercept = spearman_all$estimate, color = "red", size = 1) +
      annotate("text", x = spearman_all$estimate, y = max(table(cut(perm_cors, 30))),
               label = paste("Observed ρ =", round(spearman_all$estimate, 3)),
               hjust = -0.1, vjust = 1, color = "red") +
      labs(title = "Permutation Test: Correlation under Null",
           subtitle = paste("Empirical p-value =", round(empirical_p, 4)),
           x = "Spearman Correlation (ρ)", y = "Frequency") +
      theme_minimal()
    
    ggsave("results/figures/Figure4_Permutation_Validation.png", p4, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  }
}

cat("✓ All figures saved to results/figures/\n")

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 8: FINAL REPORT
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("SECTION 8: GENERATING FINAL REPORT\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# Create comprehensive summary
sink("results/summary/Complete_Analysis_Summary.txt")

cat("================================================================\n")
cat("COMPLETE ANALYSIS SUMMARY: Californiconus californicus\n")
cat("Testing Bergmann's Rule Across Pleistocene, Holocene, and Anthropocene\n")
cat("================================================================\n\n")

cat("DATA SUMMARY:\n")
cat("  Raw data:", nrow(Raw), "specimens\n")
cat("  Clean data:", nrow(Clean), "specimens\n")
cat("  SoCal subset:", ifelse(!is.null(SoCal), nrow(SoCal), "N/A"), "\n\n")

cat("By Epoch (Clean data):\n")
print(table(Clean$Epoch))
cat("\n")

cat("================================================================\n")
cat("BERGMANN'S RULE RESULTS\n")
cat("================================================================\n\n")
print(bergmann_results)
cat("\n")

cat("================================================================\n")
cat("SHELL SHAPE RESULTS\n")
cat("================================================================\n\n")
print(shape_results)
cat("\n")

cat("================================================================\n")
cat("VALIDATION\n")
cat("================================================================\n\n")
if(exists("empirical_p")) {
  cat("Permutation test empirical p-value:", round(empirical_p, 4), "\n")
  cat("Observed effect is", ifelse(empirical_p < 0.05, "ROBUST", "NOT robust"), "to random chance\n")
}

cat("\n")
cat("================================================================\n")
cat("OUTPUT FILES\n")
cat("================================================================\n\n")
cat("Data files:\n")
cat("  data/Data-Raw-Reference.csv\n")
cat("  data/Data_Clean.csv\n")
if(!is.null(SoCal)) cat("  data/Data_SoCal.csv\n")
cat("  data/null/ - null datasets\n\n")

cat("Results tables:\n")
cat("  results/Table1_Bergmanns_Rule.csv\n")
cat("  results/Table2_Shape_Results.csv\n")
cat("  results/TableS1_Distribution_Summary.csv\n\n")

cat("Figures:\n")
cat("  results/figures/Map1_Raw_Distribution.png\n")
cat("  results/figures/Map2_Clean_Distribution.png\n")
if(!is.null(SoCal)) cat("  results/figures/Map3_SoCal_Zoom_Density.png\n")
cat("  results/figures/Figure1_Bergmanns_Rule.png\n")
cat("  results/figures/Figure2_Shell_Shape.png\n")
cat("  results/figures/Figure3_Epoch_Boxplots.png\n")
if(exists("p4")) cat("  results/figures/Figure4_Permutation_Validation.png\n\n")

cat("Summaries:\n")
cat("  results/summary/PreAnalysis_Summary.txt\n")
cat("  results/summary/Power_Analysis_Results.txt\n")
cat("  results/summary/Complete_Analysis_Summary.txt\n")

cat("\n")
cat("================================================================\n")
cat("ANALYSIS COMPLETE\n")
cat("================================================================\n")

sink()

cat("\n✓ Final report saved to: results/summary/Complete_Analysis_Summary.txt\n")
cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("ALL ANALYSES COMPLETE - CHECK results/ FOLDER FOR OUTPUTS\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")


```

Figures and tables 
```{r}

# Set seed for reproducibility downstream
set.seed(12345)

# ==============================================================================
# DOWNSTREAM ANALYSIS: ADDRESSING RESEARCH QUESTIONS
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("MAIN ANALYSIS: ADDRESSING RESEARCH QUESTIONS\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# Load required packages
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)
library(ggpubr)
library(broom)
library(effectsize)
library(MASS)  # for robust regression
library(lme4)  # for mixed models
library(emmeans)  # for post-hoc comparisons
library(pwr)   # for power calculations

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 1: DATA PREPARATION
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== SECTION 1: DATA PREPARATION ===\n")

# Load cleaned data
if(file.exists("data/Data_Clean.csv")) {
  Clean <- read.csv("data/Data_Clean.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  cat("✓ Loaded Clean dataset from data/Data_Clean.csv\n")
} else {
  stop("Clean dataset not found. Please run data cleaning first.")
}

# Load SoCal subset
if(file.exists("data/Data_SoCal.csv")) {
  SoCal <- read.csv("data/Data_SoCal.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  cat("✓ Loaded SoCal subset from data/Data_SoCal.csv\n")
} else {
  cat("⚠ SoCal subset not found. Will create from Clean data.\n")
  socal_counties <- c("Santa Barbara", "Ventura", "Los Angeles", "Orange", "San Diego")
  SoCal <- Clean %>% filter(County %in% socal_counties)
}

# Convert Epoch to factor with correct order
Clean$Epoch <- factor(Clean$Epoch, levels = c("Pleistocene", "Holocene", "Anthropocene"))
SoCal$Epoch <- factor(SoCal$Epoch, levels = c("Pleistocene", "Holocene", "Anthropocene"))

# Create Coastal_Proximity factor (ensure it has levels)
if("Coastal_Proximity" %in% names(SoCal)) {
  SoCal$Coastal_Proximity <- factor(SoCal$Coastal_Proximity)
  cat("✓ Coastal_Proximity levels:", paste(levels(SoCal$Coastal_Proximity), collapse = ", "), "\n")
}

# Create Location factor for SoCal
if("Location" %in% names(SoCal)) {
  SoCal$Location <- factor(SoCal$Location)
  cat("✓ Location has", length(unique(SoCal$Location)), "levels\n")
}

# Display dataset summary
cat("\nDataset Summary:\n")
cat("  Total specimens:", nrow(Clean), "\n")
cat("  By epoch:", paste(names(table(Clean$Epoch)), table(Clean$Epoch), sep = "=", collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("  SoCal specimens:", nrow(SoCal), "\n")

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 2: RESEARCH QUESTION 1 - BERGMANN'S RULE (LENGTH VS LATITUDE)
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("SECTION 2: RESEARCH QUESTION 1 - BERGMANN'S RULE\n")
cat("Question: Is shell length correlated with latitude?\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2.1 OVERALL BERGMANN'S RULE TEST
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n--- 2.1 Overall Bergmann's Rule Test ---\n")
cat("Based on pre-analysis: Data is NON-NORMAL, using Spearman correlation\n")

# Spearman correlation (non-parametric, robust to non-normality)
spearman_result <- cor.test(Clean$Length_mm, Clean$Latitude, 
                            method = "spearman", 
                            exact = FALSE)

cat("\nSpearman correlation results:\n")
cat("  ρ (rho) =", round(spearman_result$estimate, 4), "\n")
cat("  p-value =", format.pval(spearman_result$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
cat("  Interpretation: ", 
    ifelse(spearman_result$p.value < 0.05, 
           "SIGNIFICANT - Bergmann's Rule supported", 
           "NOT SIGNIFICANT - Bergmann's Rule not supported"), "\n")

# Effect size interpretation
if(spearman_result$p.value < 0.05) {
  effect_strength <- ifelse(abs(spearman_result$estimate) < 0.1, "very weak",
                     ifelse(abs(spearman_result$estimate) < 0.3, "weak",
                     ifelse(abs(spearman_result$estimate) < 0.5, "moderate",
                            "strong")))
  cat("  Effect strength:", effect_strength, 
      "(ρ =", round(spearman_result$estimate, 3), ")\n")
}

# Store results
q1_results <- list()
q1_results$overall_spearman <- spearman_result

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2.2 Q1a: BERGMANN'S RULE ACROSS EPOCHS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n--- 2.2 Q1a: Bergmann's Rule Across Epochs ---\n")
cat("Testing if the relationship between length and latitude varies by epoch\n")

# Visualize first
epoch_colors <- c("Pleistocene" = "#00AFBB", 
                  "Holocene" = "#E7B800", 
                  "Anthropocene" = "#FC4E07")

q1a_plot <- ggplot(Clean, aes(x = Latitude, y = Length_mm, color = Epoch)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
  labs(title = "Bergmann's Rule Across Epochs",
       subtitle = "Testing for epoch × latitude interaction",
       x = "Latitude (°N)", y = "Shell Length (mm)",
       color = "Epoch") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave("Q1a_Bergmann_Across_Epochs.png", q1a_plot, 
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

cat("✓ Visualization saved to: Q1a_Bergmann_Across_Epochs.png\n")

# Method 1: Robust regression (less sensitive to non-normality)
cat("\nRobust regression (MASS::rlm) - recommended for non-normal data:\n")
rlm_epoch <- tryCatch({
  rlm(Length_mm ~ Latitude * Epoch, data = Clean, maxit = 100)
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("⚠ Robust regression failed:", e$message, "\n")
  return(NULL)
})

if(!is.null(rlm_epoch)) {
  rlm_summary <- summary(rlm_epoch)
  print(rlm_summary)
  
  # Try to get interaction p-value using F-test
  tryCatch({
    # Compare models with and without interaction
    rlm_main <- rlm(Length_mm ~ Latitude + Epoch, data = Clean, maxit = 100)
    
    # Calculate F-statistic manually
    rss_full <- sum(residuals(rlm_epoch)^2)
    rss_reduced <- sum(residuals(rlm_main)^2)
    df_full <- length(coef(rlm_epoch))
    df_reduced <- length(coef(rlm_main))
    n <- nrow(Clean)
    
    f_stat <- ((rss_reduced - rss_full) / (df_full - df_reduced)) / 
              (rss_full / (n - df_full))
    interaction_p <- pf(f_stat, df_full - df_reduced, n - df_full, lower.tail = FALSE)
    
    cat("\nInteraction test (Latitude:Epoch):\n")
    cat("  F-statistic =", round(f_stat, 4), "\n")
    cat("  p-value =", format.pval(interaction_p, digits = 3), "\n")
    cat("  Interpretation: ", 
        ifelse(interaction_p < 0.05, 
               "SIGNIFICANT - Relationship varies by epoch", 
               "NOT SIGNIFICANT - Relationship consistent across epochs"), "\n")
    
    q1_results$interaction_p <- interaction_p
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("⚠ Could not calculate interaction p-value:", e$message, "\n")
    interaction_p <- NA
    q1_results$interaction_p <- NA
  })
} else {
  interaction_p <- NA
  q1_results$interaction_p <- NA
}

# Method 2: Spearman correlations by epoch (always works)
cat("\nSpearman correlations by epoch:\n")
epoch_spearman <- list()
for(epoch in levels(Clean$Epoch)) {
  epoch_data <- Clean %>% filter(Epoch == epoch)
  if(nrow(epoch_data) > 10) {
    sp <- tryCatch({
      cor.test(epoch_data$Length_mm, epoch_data$Latitude, 
               method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("  ⚠ Spearman failed for", epoch, ":", e$message, "\n")
      return(NULL)
    })
    
    if(!is.null(sp)) {
      epoch_spearman[[epoch]] <- sp
      cat("  ", epoch, ": ρ =", round(sp$estimate, 4), 
          ", p =", format.pval(sp$p.value, digits = 3), 
          ifelse(sp$p.value < 0.05, " *", ""), "\n")
    }
  }
}

# Store results
q1_results$rlm_epoch <- rlm_epoch
q1_results$epoch_spearman <- epoch_spearman

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2.3 Q1b: SOCAL SUBSET - MAINLAND VS ISLAND EFFECTS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n--- 2.3 Q1b: SoCal Subset - Mainland vs Island Effects ---\n")

# Check if Coastal_Proximity has sufficient levels
if("Coastal_Proximity" %in% names(SoCal) && 
   length(unique(na.omit(SoCal$Coastal_Proximity))) > 1) {
  
  cat("\nTesting mainland vs island differences in SoCal region\n")
  
  # Visualize
  q1b_plot <- ggplot(SoCal, aes(x = Latitude, y = Length_mm, 
                                 color = Coastal_Proximity)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1.5) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
    facet_wrap(~ Epoch, nrow = 1) +
    labs(title = "SoCal: Mainland vs Island Comparison",
         x = "Latitude (°N)", y = "Shell Length (mm)",
         color = "Coastal Proximity") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
  ggsave("Q1b_SoCal_Mainland_vs_Island.png", q1b_plot,
         width = 14, height = 5, dpi = 300)
  
  # Mixed effects model with Location as random effect
  cat("\nMixed Effects Model (Location as random effect):\n")
  cat("Formula: Length_mm ~ Latitude + Coastal_Proximity + (1|Location)\n")
  
  # Check if Location has sufficient levels
  if("Location" %in% names(SoCal) && length(unique(SoCal$Location)) > 1) {
    lmer_model <- tryCatch({
      lmer(Length_mm ~ Latitude + Coastal_Proximity + (1|Location), data = SoCal)
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("⚠ Mixed model failed:", e$message, "\n")
      return(NULL)
    })
    
    if(!is.null(lmer_model)) {
      print(summary(lmer_model))
      
      # Test fixed effects
      cat("\nFixed effects tests:\n")
      print(anova(lmer_model))
      
      # Pairwise comparisons
      cat("\nEstimated marginal means:\n")
      emmeans_result <- tryCatch({
        emmeans(lmer_model, ~ Coastal_Proximity)
      }, error = function(e) {
        cat("⚠ emmeans failed:", e$message, "\n")
        return(NULL)
      })
      
      if(!is.null(emmeans_result)) {
        print(emmeans_result)
        
        # Pairwise contrast
        pairs_result <- pairs(emmeans_result)
        print(pairs_result)
        q1_results$socal_emmeans <- emmeans_result
      }
      
      q1_results$socal_mixed <- lmer_model
    }
  } else {
    cat("⚠ Insufficient Location levels for mixed model. Using regular ANCOVA.\n")
    lm_socal <- lm(Length_mm ~ Latitude * Coastal_Proximity, data = SoCal)
    print(summary(lm_socal))
    q1_results$socal_lm <- lm_socal
  }
  
} else {
  cat("⚠ Coastal_Proximity variable not available or has insufficient levels.\n")
  cat("   Cannot test mainland vs island differences.\n")
}

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 3: RESEARCH QUESTION 2 - SHELL SHAPE (GLOBOSITY VS LATITUDE)
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("SECTION 3: RESEARCH QUESTION 2 - SHELL SHAPE (GLOBOSITY)\n")
cat("Question: Is shell shape (globosity) correlated with latitude?\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3.1 OVERALL SHAPE VS LATITUDE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n--- 3.1 Overall Shape vs Latitude ---\n")

# Spearman correlation (non-parametric)
spearman_shape <- cor.test(Clean$Globosity_WoverL, Clean$Latitude, 
                           method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)

cat("\nSpearman correlation results (Globosity ~ Latitude):\n")
cat("  ρ (rho) =", round(spearman_shape$estimate, 4), "\n")
cat("  p-value =", format.pval(spearman_shape$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
cat("  Interpretation: ", 
    ifelse(spearman_shape$p.value < 0.05, 
           "SIGNIFICANT - Shape varies with latitude", 
           "NOT SIGNIFICANT - No latitudinal pattern"), "\n")

q2_results <- list()
q2_results$overall_spearman <- spearman_shape

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3.2 Q2a: SHAPE VS LATITUDE ACROSS EPOCHS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n--- 3.2 Q2a: Shape vs Latitude Across Epochs ---\n")

# Visualize
q2a_plot <- ggplot(Clean, aes(x = Latitude, y = Globosity_WoverL, color = Epoch)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
  labs(title = "Shell Shape (Globosity) Across Latitude and Epoch",
       x = "Latitude (°N)", y = "Globosity (W/L ratio)",
       color = "Epoch") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave("Q2a_Shape_Across_Epochs.png", q2a_plot, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Robust regression for shape
rlm_shape <- tryCatch({
  rlm(Globosity_WoverL ~ Latitude * Epoch, data = Clean, maxit = 100)
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("⚠ Robust regression for shape failed:", e$message, "\n")
  return(NULL)
})

if(!is.null(rlm_shape)) {
  cat("\nRobust regression results (shape ~ latitude * epoch):\n")
  print(summary(rlm_shape))
  
  # Test interaction
  tryCatch({
    rlm_shape_main <- rlm(Globosity_WoverL ~ Latitude + Epoch, data = Clean, maxit = 100)
    
    rss_full <- sum(residuals(rlm_shape)^2)
    rss_reduced <- sum(residuals(rlm_shape_main)^2)
    df_full <- length(coef(rlm_shape))
    df_reduced <- length(coef(rlm_shape_main))
    n <- nrow(Clean)
    
    f_stat <- ((rss_reduced - rss_full) / (df_full - df_reduced)) / 
              (rss_full / (n - df_full))
    shape_interaction_p <- pf(f_stat, df_full - df_reduced, n - df_full, lower.tail = FALSE)
    
    cat("\nInteraction test (Latitude:Epoch): p =", 
        format.pval(shape_interaction_p, digits = 3), "\n")
    cat("Interpretation: ", 
        ifelse(shape_interaction_p < 0.05, 
               "SIGNIFICANT - Shape-latitude relationship varies by epoch", 
               "NOT SIGNIFICANT - Relationship consistent across epochs"), "\n")
    
    q2_results$shape_interaction_p <- shape_interaction_p
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("⚠ Could not calculate shape interaction p-value:", e$message, "\n")
    q2_results$shape_interaction_p <- NA
  })
}

# Spearman by epoch
cat("\nSpearman correlations by epoch:\n")
epoch_shape <- list()
for(epoch in levels(Clean$Epoch)) {
  epoch_data <- Clean %>% filter(Epoch == epoch)
  if(nrow(epoch_data) > 10) {
    sp <- tryCatch({
      cor.test(epoch_data$Globosity_WoverL, epoch_data$Latitude, 
               method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("  ⚠ Spearman failed for", epoch, ":", e$message, "\n")
      return(NULL)
    })
    
    if(!is.null(sp)) {
      epoch_shape[[epoch]] <- sp
      cat("  ", epoch, ": ρ =", round(sp$estimate, 4), 
          ", p =", format.pval(sp$p.value, digits = 3), 
          ifelse(sp$p.value < 0.05, " *", ""), "\n")
    }
  }
}

q2_results$rlm_shape <- rlm_shape
q2_results$epoch_shape <- epoch_shape

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3.3 Q2b: SOCAL SHAPE - MAINLAND VS ISLAND
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n--- 3.3 Q2b: SoCal Shape - Mainland vs Island Effects ---\n")

if("Coastal_Proximity" %in% names(SoCal) && 
   length(unique(na.omit(SoCal$Coastal_Proximity))) > 1) {
  
  # Visualize
  q2b_plot <- ggplot(SoCal, aes(x = Latitude, y = Globosity_WoverL, 
                                 color = Coastal_Proximity)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1.5) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
    facet_wrap(~ Epoch, nrow = 1) +
    labs(title = "SoCal: Shape Comparison - Mainland vs Island",
         x = "Latitude (°N)", y = "Globosity (W/L)",
         color = "Coastal Proximity") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
  ggsave("Q2b_SoCal_Shape_Mainland_vs_Island.png", q2b_plot,
         width = 14, height = 5, dpi = 300)
  
  # Mixed model for shape
  if("Location" %in% names(SoCal) && length(unique(SoCal$Location)) > 1) {
    lmer_shape <- tryCatch({
      lmer(Globosity_WoverL ~ Latitude + Coastal_Proximity + (1|Location), data = SoCal)
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("⚠ Mixed model for shape failed:", e$message, "\n")
      return(NULL)
    })
    
    if(!is.null(lmer_shape)) {
      cat("\nMixed Effects Model for shape:\n")
      print(summary(lmer_shape))
      
      # Test fixed effects
      print(anova(lmer_shape))
      
      q2_results$socal_shape_mixed <- lmer_shape
    }
  }
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3.4 Q2c: MUSEUM VS DIGITAL COMPARISON
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n--- 3.4 Q2c: Museum vs Digital Comparison ---\n")
cat("Note: Your cleaned dataset contains only Museum/Field samples.\n")
cat("      To properly test museum vs digital, iNaturalist data would need to be included.\n")

if("Data_Type" %in% names(Clean) && length(unique(Clean$Data_Type)) > 1) {
  cat("\nComparing shape between Museum/Field and iNaturalist samples:\n")
  
  # Visual comparison
  q2c_plot <- ggplot(Clean, aes(x = Data_Type, y = Globosity_WoverL, fill = Data_Type)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
    facet_wrap(~ Epoch) +
    labs(title = "Shape Comparison: Museum vs Digital Samples",
         x = "Data Source", y = "Globosity (W/L)") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
  
  ggsave("Q2c_Museum_vs_Digital.png", q2c_plot, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  
  # Statistical test
  wilcox_test <- wilcox.test(Globosity_WoverL ~ Data_Type, data = Clean)
  cat("\nWilcoxon rank-sum test (non-parametric):\n")
  cat("  p-value =", format.pval(wilcox_test$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
  
  # With time as covariate
  cat("\nTesting with Year as covariate:\n")
  lm_time <- lm(Globosity_WoverL ~ Data_Type + Year, data = Clean)
  print(summary(lm_time))
  
  q2_results$museum_vs_digital <- list(
    wilcox = wilcox_test,
    lm_time = lm_time
  )
  
} else {
  cat("\n⚠ Only Museum/Field data present in cleaned dataset.\n")
  cat("   To address Q2c, you would need to:\n")
  cat("   1. Re-run data cleaning without filtering out iNaturalist samples\n")
  cat("   2. Or load the original raw data and compare data sources\n")
  
  # Create placeholder note
  q2_results$museum_vs_digital <- "Not tested - only Museum/Field data available"
}

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 4: SUMMARY TABLES AND FIGURES
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("SECTION 4: SUMMARY TABLES AND FIGURES\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4.1 SUMMARY TABLE FOR Q1 (BERGMANN'S RULE)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Create summary table for Bergmann's Rule results
bergmann_table <- data.frame(
  Test = c("Overall Spearman", 
           "Pleistocene", 
           "Holocene", 
           "Anthropocene",
           "Epoch Interaction"),
  Correlation = c(
    round(spearman_result$estimate, 3),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_spearman$Pleistocene), round(epoch_spearman$Pleistocene$estimate, 3), NA),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_spearman$Holocene), round(epoch_spearman$Holocene$estimate, 3), NA),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_spearman$Anthropocene), round(epoch_spearman$Anthropocene$estimate, 3), NA),
    NA
  ),
  P_value = c(
    format.pval(spearman_result$p.value, digits = 3),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_spearman$Pleistocene), format.pval(epoch_spearman$Pleistocene$p.value, digits = 3), NA),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_spearman$Holocene), format.pval(epoch_spearman$Holocene$p.value, digits = 3), NA),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_spearman$Anthropocene), format.pval(epoch_spearman$Anthropocene$p.value, digits = 3), NA),
    ifelse(!is.null(q1_results$interaction_p) && !is.na(q1_results$interaction_p), 
           format.pval(q1_results$interaction_p, digits = 3), "NA")
  ),
  Significant = c(
    ifelse(spearman_result$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_spearman$Pleistocene) && epoch_spearman$Pleistocene$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_spearman$Holocene) && epoch_spearman$Holocene$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_spearman$Anthropocene) && epoch_spearman$Anthropocene$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
    ifelse(!is.null(q1_results$interaction_p) && !is.na(q1_results$interaction_p) && q1_results$interaction_p < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
  ),
  Interpretation = c(
    ifelse(spearman_result$p.value < 0.05, 
           "Supports Bergmann's Rule", 
           "No support"),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_spearman$Pleistocene) && epoch_spearman$Pleistocene$p.value < 0.05, 
           "Supports", "No support"),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_spearman$Holocene) && epoch_spearman$Holocene$p.value < 0.05, 
           "Supports", "No support"),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_spearman$Anthropocene) && epoch_spearman$Anthropocene$p.value < 0.05, 
           "Supports", "No support"),
    ifelse(!is.null(q1_results$interaction_p) && !is.na(q1_results$interaction_p) && q1_results$interaction_p < 0.05, 
           "Relationship varies by epoch", 
           "Consistent across epochs")
  )
)

write.csv(bergmann_table, "Table1_Bergmanns_Rule_Results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n✓ Table 1 saved to: Table1_Bergmanns_Rule_Results.csv\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4.2 SUMMARY TABLE FOR Q2 (SHAPE)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

shape_table <- data.frame(
  Test = c("Overall Shape Spearman", 
           "Pleistocene", 
           "Holocene", 
           "Anthropocene",
           "Epoch Interaction"),
  Correlation = c(
    round(spearman_shape$estimate, 3),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_shape$Pleistocene), round(epoch_shape$Pleistocene$estimate, 3), NA),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_shape$Holocene), round(epoch_shape$Holocene$estimate, 3), NA),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_shape$Anthropocene), round(epoch_shape$Anthropocene$estimate, 3), NA),
    NA
  ),
  P_value = c(
    format.pval(spearman_shape$p.value, digits = 3),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_shape$Pleistocene), format.pval(epoch_shape$Pleistocene$p.value, digits = 3), NA),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_shape$Holocene), format.pval(epoch_shape$Holocene$p.value, digits = 3), NA),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_shape$Anthropocene), format.pval(epoch_shape$Anthropocene$p.value, digits = 3), NA),
    ifelse(!is.null(q2_results$shape_interaction_p) && !is.na(q2_results$shape_interaction_p), 
           format.pval(q2_results$shape_interaction_p, digits = 3), "NA")
  ),
  Significant = c(
    ifelse(spearman_shape$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_shape$Pleistocene) && epoch_shape$Pleistocene$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_shape$Holocene) && epoch_shape$Holocene$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_shape$Anthropocene) && epoch_shape$Anthropocene$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
    ifelse(!is.null(q2_results$shape_interaction_p) && !is.na(q2_results$shape_interaction_p) && q2_results$shape_interaction_p < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
  ),
  Interpretation = c(
    ifelse(spearman_shape$p.value < 0.05, 
           "Shape varies with latitude", 
           "No latitudinal pattern"),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_shape$Pleistocene) && epoch_shape$Pleistocene$p.value < 0.05, 
           "Varies", "No pattern"),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_shape$Holocene) && epoch_shape$Holocene$p.value < 0.05, 
           "Varies", "No pattern"),
    ifelse(!is.null(epoch_shape$Anthropocene) && epoch_shape$Anthropocene$p.value < 0.05, 
           "Varies", "No pattern"),
    ifelse(!is.null(q2_results$shape_interaction_p) && !is.na(q2_results$shape_interaction_p) && q2_results$shape_interaction_p < 0.05, 
           "Pattern varies by epoch", 
           "Consistent across epochs")
  )
)

write.csv(shape_table, "Table2_Shape_Results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Table 2 saved to: Table2_Shape_Results.csv\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4.3 COMBINED VISUALIZATION: FOUR-PANEL FIGURE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Create comprehensive figure for manuscript
cat("\nCreating comprehensive four-panel figure...\n")

# Panel A: Bergmann's Rule (Length ~ Latitude)
pA <- ggplot(Clean, aes(x = Latitude, y = Length_mm, color = Epoch)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
  labs(title = "A) Bergmann's Rule", 
       x = "Latitude (°N)", y = "Shell Length (mm)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Panel B: Shape (Globosity ~ Latitude)
pB <- ggplot(Clean, aes(x = Latitude, y = Globosity_WoverL, color = Epoch)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
```
