```{r}
  labs(title = "B) Shell Shape", 
       x = "Latitude (°N)", y = "Globosity (W/L)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Panel C: Epoch comparison (boxplots)
pC <- ggplot(Clean, aes(x = Epoch, y = Length_mm, fill = Epoch)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
  labs(title = "C) Size by Epoch", 
       x = "Epoch", y = "Shell Length (mm)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Panel D: SoCal comparison (if available)
if("Coastal_Proximity" %in% names(SoCal) && 
   length(unique(na.omit(SoCal$Coastal_Proximity))) > 1) {
  pD <- ggplot(SoCal, aes(x = Coastal_Proximity, y = Length_mm, fill = Coastal_Proximity)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
    facet_wrap(~ Epoch) +
    labs(title = "D) SoCal: Mainland vs Island", 
         x = "Coastal Proximity", y = "Shell Length (mm)") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
} else {
  pD <- ggplot() + 
    annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, label = "Insufficient data for SoCal comparison") +
    theme_void() +
    labs(title = "D) SoCal comparison unavailable")
}

# Combine panels
figure4 <- plot_grid(pA, pB, pC, pD, ncol = 2, labels = "AUTO")
ggsave("Figure4_Combined_Results.png", figure4, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
cat("✓ Combined figure saved to: Figure4_Combined_Results.png\n")

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 5: VALIDATION AGAINST NULL DATASETS
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("SECTION 5: VALIDATION AGAINST NULL DATASETS\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")

# Load null datasets if they exist
if(file.exists("data/NullDataset_Complete.csv")) {
  
  null_complete <- read.csv("data/NullDataset_Complete.csv")
  
  # Test correlation in null dataset
  null_cor <- tryCatch({
    cor.test(null_complete$Length_mm, null_complete$Latitude, 
             method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("⚠ Null correlation test failed:", e$message, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  if(!is.null(null_cor)) {
    cat("\nValidation against completely randomized null dataset:\n")
    cat("  Observed ρ =", round(spearman_result$estimate, 4), "\n")
    cat("  Null ρ =", round(null_cor$estimate, 4), "\n")
    cat("  Null p-value =", format.pval(null_cor$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
    cat("  Validation: ", 
        ifelse(abs(spearman_result$estimate) > abs(null_cor$estimate) * 2,
               "Observed effect is robust", 
               "Observed effect may be influenced by random variation"), "\n")
  }
  
  # Load permutation distributions
  if(file.exists("data/Permutation_Distributions.csv")) {
    perm_dist <- tryCatch({
      read.csv("data/Permutation_Distributions.csv")
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("⚠ Could not load permutation distributions:", e$message, "\n")
      return(NULL)
    })
    
    if(!is.null(perm_dist) && nrow(perm_dist) > 0) {
      # Calculate empirical p-value
      empirical_p <- mean(abs(perm_dist$correlation) >= abs(spearman_result$estimate), na.rm = TRUE)
      cat("\nPermutation test empirical p-value:", empirical_p, "\n")
      
      # Plot permutation distribution
      png("Permutation_Distribution.png", width = 8, height = 6, units = "in", res = 300)
      hist(perm_dist$correlation, breaks = 30, 
           main = "Permutation Distribution of Correlations",
           xlab = "Spearman ρ", col = "lightgray")
      abline(v = spearman_result$estimate, col = "red", lwd = 2)
      abline(v = -spearman_result$estimate, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
      legend("topright", legend = c("Observed ρ", "Null distribution"), 
             col = c("red", "black"), lwd = c(2, 1))
      dev.off()
      cat("✓ Permutation distribution plot saved\n")
    }
  }
}

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 6: SUMMARY OF FINDINGS (WITH PROPER NA HANDLING)
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("SECTION 6: SUMMARY OF FINDINGS\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")

cat("\n=== RESEARCH QUESTION 1: BERGMANN'S RULE ===\n")
cat("Q1: Is shell length correlated with latitude?\n")
if(spearman_result$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("  ✓ YES - Significant correlation detected\n")
  cat("     ρ =", round(spearman_result$estimate, 4), 
      ", p =", format.pval(spearman_result$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
} else {
  cat("  ✗ NO - No significant correlation detected\n")
}

cat("\nQ1a: Does this remain true across Epochs?\n")
# Safely check interaction_p
interaction_exists <- !is.null(q1_results$interaction_p) && !is.na(q1_results$interaction_p)

if(interaction_exists) {
  if(q1_results$interaction_p < 0.05) {
    cat("  ✓ Relationship VARIES significantly by epoch\n")
  } else {
    cat("  ✓ Relationship is CONSISTENT across epochs\n")
  }
  cat("  Interaction p-value =", format.pval(q1_results$interaction_p, digits = 3), "\n")
} else {
  cat("  ⚠ Could not calculate interaction p-value\n")
  cat("  Using epoch-specific correlations for interpretation:\n")
}

cat("  Epoch-specific correlations:\n")
for(epoch in names(epoch_spearman)) {
  if(!is.null(epoch_spearman[[epoch]])) {
    cat("    ", epoch, ": ρ =", round(epoch_spearman[[epoch]]$estimate, 4), 
        ifelse(epoch_spearman[[epoch]]$p.value < 0.05, "*", ""), "\n")
  }
}

cat("\nQ1b: Are there confounding factors in SoCal (mainland vs islands)?\n")
if(!is.null(q1_results$socal_mixed)) {
  cat("  ✓ Mixed model analysis completed\n")
  cat("     Check Table1_SoCal_Results.csv for details\n")
} else if(!is.null(q1_results$socal_lm)) {
  cat("  ✓ ANCOVA analysis completed\n")
  cat("     Check Table1_SoCal_Results.csv for details\n")
} else {
  cat("  ⚠ Insufficient data for mainland vs island comparison\n")
}

cat("\n=== RESEARCH QUESTION 2: SHELL SHAPE ===\n")
cat("Q2: Is shell shape (globosity) correlated with latitude?\n")
if(spearman_shape$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("  ✓ YES - Significant correlation detected\n")
  cat("     ρ =", round(spearman_shape$estimate, 4), 
      ", p =", format.pval(spearman_shape$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
} else {
  cat("  ✗ NO - No significant correlation detected\n")
}

cat("\nQ2a: Is this pattern consistent across Epochs?\n")
shape_interaction_exists <- !is.null(q2_results$shape_interaction_p) && !is.na(q2_results$shape_interaction_p)

if(shape_interaction_exists) {
  if(q2_results$shape_interaction_p < 0.05) {
    cat("  ✓ Shape-latitude relationship VARIES by epoch\n")
  } else {
    cat("  ✓ Relationship is CONSISTENT across epochs\n")
  }
  cat("  Interaction p-value =", format.pval(q2_results$shape_interaction_p, digits = 3), "\n")
} else {
  cat("  ⚠ Could not calculate interaction p-value\n")
  cat("  Using epoch-specific correlations for interpretation:\n")
}

cat("  Epoch-specific shape correlations:\n")
for(epoch in names(epoch_shape)) {
  if(!is.null(epoch_shape[[epoch]])) {
    cat("    ", epoch, ": ρ =", round(epoch_shape[[epoch]]$estimate, 4), 
        ifelse(epoch_shape[[epoch]]$p.value < 0.05, "*", ""), "\n")
  }
}

cat("\nQ2c: Museum vs Digital comparison?\n")
if(is.character(q2_results$museum_vs_digital)) {
  cat("  ⚠", q2_results$museum_vs_digital, "\n")
} else if(!is.null(q2_results$museum_vs_digital)) {
  cat("  ✓ Comparison completed\n")
  if(!is.null(q2_results$museum_vs_digital$wilcox)) {
    cat("     Wilcoxon test p =", 
        format.pval(q2_results$museum_vs_digital$wilcox$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("  ⚠ Museum vs digital comparison not performed\n")
}

cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("ANALYSIS COMPLETE - Results saved to working directory\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")

# Save all results
all_results <- list(
  q1 = q1_results,
  q2 = q2_results,
  bergmann_table = bergmann_table,
  shape_table = shape_table,
  timestamp = Sys.time()
)

saveRDS(all_results, "Complete_Analysis_Results.rds")
cat("\n✓ Complete results saved to: Complete_Analysis_Results.rds\n")

# List all output files
cat("\nOutput files generated:\n")
cat("  1. Q1a_Bergmann_Across_Epochs.png\n")
if(file.exists("Q1b_SoCal_Mainland_vs_Island.png")) cat("  2. Q1b_SoCal_Mainland_vs_Island.png\n")
cat("  3. Q2a_Shape_Across_Epochs.png\n")
if(file.exists("Q2b_SoCal_Shape_Mainland_vs_Island.png")) cat("  4. Q2b_SoCal_Shape_Mainland_vs_Island.png\n")
if(file.exists("Q2c_Museum_vs_Digital.png")) cat("  5. Q2c_Museum_vs_Digital.png\n")
cat("  6. Figure4_Combined_Results.png\n")
cat("  7. Table1_Bergmanns_Rule_Results.csv\n")
cat("  8. Table2_Shape_Results.csv\n")
if(file.exists("Permutation_Distribution.png")) cat("  9. Permutation_Distribution.png\n")
cat("  10. Complete_Analysis_Results.rds\n")


```



```{r}
# Set seed for reproducibility downstream
set.seed(12345)

# ==============================================================================
# 2. SIMPLIFIED MAP CREATION
# ==============================================================================

# Function to create simple maps without requiring shapefiles
create_simple_maps <- function(data) {
  
  cat("\n=== CREATING SIMPLE MAPS ===\n")
  
  # Remove geometry if it's an sf object
  if(inherits(data, "sf")) {
    data_df <- as.data.frame(data)
    # Remove geometry column if it exists
    if("geometry" %in% names(data_df)) {
      data_df$geometry <- NULL
    }
  } else {
    data_df <- data
  }
  
  # Check for required columns
  if(!all(c("Longitude", "Latitude", "Epoch") %in% names(data_df))) {
    cat("Warning: Missing required columns for mapping\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Define epoch colors
  epoch_colors <- c("Pleistocene" = "#00AFBB", 
                    "Holocene" = "#E7B800", 
                    "Anthropocene" = "#FC4E07")
  
  # Get California and Mexico map data
  cat("Loading map data...\n")
  
  # Get world data
  world <- map_data("world")
  
  # Filter for relevant regions
  study_area <- world %>%
    filter((region %in% c("USA", "Canada", "Mexico") & 
             long >= -130 & long <= -110 & lat >= 15 & lat <= 50) |
           (region == "Mexico" & long >= -130 & long <= -110))
  
  # Create base map
  base_map <- ggplot() +
    geom_polygon(data = study_area,
                 aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group),
                 fill = "gray90", color = "gray70", size = 0.2) +
    coord_fixed(xlim = c(-125, -110), ylim = c(22, 42), ratio = 1.3) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
    )
  
  # Figure 1A: All collection points
  fig1a <- base_map +
    geom_point(data = data_df,
               aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, color = Epoch),
               alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
    scale_color_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
    labs(title = "All Collection Locations",
         x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude",
         color = "Epoch") +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
  # Figure 1B: With density contours
  fig1b <- base_map +
    stat_density_2d(data = data_df,
                    aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, fill = ..level..),
                    geom = "polygon", alpha = 0.3, bins = 20) +
    geom_point(data = data_df,
               aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, color = Epoch),
               alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
    scale_color_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red", guide = "none") +
    labs(title = "Collection Density",
         x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude",
         color = "Epoch") +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
  # Combine plots
  figure1 <- plot_grid(fig1a, fig1b, 
                       labels = c("A", "B"), 
                       label_size = 14,
                       ncol = 2)
  
  # Save figure
  ggsave("Figure1_Collection_Maps.png", figure1, 
         width = 14, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
  
  cat("✓ Figure 1 saved to: Figure1_Collection_Maps.png\n")
  
  return(list(figure1 = figure1))
}

# ==============================================================================
# 3. CORE ANALYSIS FUNCTIONS (COMPATIBLE WITH OLDER R VERSIONS)
# ==============================================================================

# Function to test statistical assumptions
test_assumptions <- function(data) {
  
  cat("\n=== TESTING STATISTICAL ASSUMPTIONS ===\n")
  
  # Remove geometry if data is sf
  if(inherits(data, "sf")) {
    data_df <- as.data.frame(data)
    # Remove geometry column if it exists
    if("geometry" %in% names(data_df)) {
      data_df$geometry <- NULL
    }
  } else {
    data_df <- data
  }
  
  results <- list()
  
  # Check for required variables
  if(!"Length_mm" %in% names(data_df)) {
    cat("Warning: Length_mm not found in data\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # 1. Normality test
  cat("\n1. Normality test for Length_mm:\n")
  shapiro_test <- shapiro.test(data_df$Length_mm)
  cat("Shapiro-Wilk: W =", round(shapiro_test$statistic, 4),
      "p =", format.pval(shapiro_test$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
  
  is_normal <- shapiro_test$p.value > 0.05
  cat("Data is", ifelse(is_normal, "NORMALLY", "NOT NORMALLY"), "distributed\n")
  
  # 2. Homogeneity of variance by epoch
  if("Epoch" %in% names(data_df) && length(unique(data_df$Epoch)) > 1) {
    cat("\n2. Homogeneity of variance by epoch:\n")
    levene_test <- leveneTest(Length_mm ~ Epoch, data = data_df)
    cat("Levene's test: F =", round(levene_test$`F value`[1], 4),
        "p =", format.pval(levene_test$`Pr(>F)`[1], digits = 3), "\n")
    
    equal_var <- levene_test$`Pr(>F)`[1] > 0.05
    cat("Variances are", ifelse(equal_var, "EQUAL", "UNEQUAL"), "across epochs\n")
  }
  
  # 3. Create diagnostic plots
  cat("\n3. Creating diagnostic plots...\n")
  
  # Histogram
  hist_plot <- ggplot(data_df, aes(x = Length_mm)) +
    geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..), bins = 30, fill = "lightblue", color = "black") +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5, fill = "red") +
    labs(title = "Distribution of Shell Length",
         x = "Length (mm)", y = "Density") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # QQ plot
  qq_data <- data.frame(
    Theoretical = qqnorm(data_df$Length_mm, plot.it = FALSE)$x,
    Sample = qqnorm(data_df$Length_mm, plot.it = FALSE)$y
  )
  
  qq_plot <- ggplot(qq_data, aes(x = Theoretical, y = Sample)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_qq_line(aes(sample = Sample), color = "red") +
    labs(title = "Normal Q-Q Plot",
         x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Sample Quantiles") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # Save diagnostic plots
  diagnostic_plots <- plot_grid(hist_plot, qq_plot, ncol = 2)
  ggsave("Figure_Diagnostics.png", diagnostic_plots, 
         width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 300)
  
  cat("✓ Diagnostic plots saved to: Figure_Diagnostics.png\n")
  
  return(results)
}

# Function to analyze Bergmann's Rule
analyze_bergmanns_rule <- function(data) {
  
  cat("\n=== ANALYZING BERGMANN'S RULE ===\n")
  
  # Remove geometry if data is sf
  if(inherits(data, "sf")) {
    data_df <- as.data.frame(data)
    # Remove geometry column if it exists
    if("geometry" %in% names(data_df)) {
      data_df$geometry <- NULL
    }
  } else {
    data_df <- data
  }
  
  # Check required columns
  required <- c("Length_mm", "Latitude", "Epoch")
  missing <- setdiff(required, names(data_df))
  
  if(length(missing) > 0) {
    cat("Warning: Missing required columns:", paste(missing, collapse = ", "), "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  results <- list()
  
  # 1. Overall relationship
  cat("\n1. Overall relationship: Length ~ Latitude\n")
  lm_overall <- lm(Length_mm ~ Latitude, data = data_df)
  summary_overall <- summary(lm_overall)
  
  cat("Intercept:", round(coef(lm_overall)[1], 4), "\n")
  cat("Slope:", round(coef(lm_overall)[2], 4), "mm per degree latitude\n")
  cat("R-squared:", round(summary_overall$r.squared, 4), "\n")
  cat("Adjusted R-squared:", round(summary_overall$adj.r.squared, 4), "\n")
  cat("p-value:", format.pval(summary_overall$coefficients[2, 4], digits = 3), "\n")
  
  # 2. By epoch
  cat("\n2. By epoch: Length ~ Latitude * Epoch\n")
  lm_epoch <- lm(Length_mm ~ Latitude * Epoch, data = data_df)
  anova_epoch <- anova(lm_epoch)
  print(anova_epoch)
  
  # 3. Separate models for each epoch
  cat("\n3. Separate models for each epoch:\n")
  epoch_models <- list()
  
  for(epoch in levels(data_df$Epoch)) {
    epoch_data <- data_df %>% filter(Epoch == epoch)
    
    if(nrow(epoch_data) > 10) {  # Need sufficient data
      lm_epoch_specific <- lm(Length_mm ~ Latitude, data = epoch_data)
      epoch_models[[epoch]] <- lm_epoch_specific
      
      cat("\n", epoch, ":\n", sep = "")
      cat("  n =", nrow(epoch_data), "\n")
      cat("  Slope =", round(coef(lm_epoch_specific)[2], 4), "\n")
      cat("  R² =", round(summary(lm_epoch_specific)$r.squared, 4), "\n")
      cat("  p =", format.pval(summary(lm_epoch_specific)$coefficients[2, 4], digits = 3), "\n")
    }
  }
  
  # 4. Create Bergmann's Rule plot
  cat("\n4. Creating Bergmann's Rule visualization...\n")
  
  epoch_colors <- c("Pleistocene" = "#00AFBB", 
                    "Holocene" = "#E7B800", 
                    "Anthropocene" = "#FC4E07")
  
  bergmann_plot <- ggplot(data_df, aes(x = Latitude, y = Length_mm, color = Epoch)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1.5) +
    geom_smooth(aes(fill = Epoch), method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", se = FALSE, size = 0.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = epoch_colors) +
    labs(
      title = "Bergmann's Rule in Californiconus californicus",
      x = "Latitude (°N)",
      y = "Shell Length (mm)",
      subtitle = paste("Overall slope =", round(coef(lm_overall)[2], 4), 
                      "mm/degree, R² =", round(summary_overall$r.squared, 3))
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
  
  # Save plot
  ggsave("Figure_Bergmanns_Rule.png", bergmann_plot, 
         width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
  
  cat("✓ Bergmann's Rule plot saved to: Figure_Bergmanns_Rule.png\n")
  
  # 5. Calculate effect sizes
  cat("\n5. Calculating effect sizes...\n")
  
  # Split data by median latitude
  lat_median <- median(data_df$Latitude, na.rm = TRUE)
  data_df$Lat_group <- ifelse(data_df$Latitude > lat_median, "High", "Low")
  
  if(length(unique(data_df$Lat_group)) > 1) {
    cohen_d_result <- cohens_d(Length_mm ~ Lat_group, data = data_df)
    cat("Cohen's d for latitude effect:", round(cohen_d_result$Cohens_d, 3), "\n")
    results$cohen_d <- cohen_d_result
  }
  
  # Store results
  results$models <- list(
    overall = lm_overall,
    epoch_interaction = lm_epoch,
    epoch_specific = epoch_models
  )
  
  results$plot <- bergmann_plot
  
  return(results)
}

# Function to create correlation matrix (compatible with older R)
create_correlation_analysis <- function(data) {
  
  cat("\n=== CREATING CORRELATION ANALYSIS ===\n")
  
  # Remove geometry if data is sf
  if(inherits(data, "sf")) {
    data_df <- as.data.frame(data)
    # Remove geometry column if it exists
    if("geometry" %in% names(data_df)) {
      data_df$geometry <- NULL
    }
  } else {
    data_df <- data
  }
  
  # Select numeric variables (compatible way for older R)
  # Identify numeric columns
  numeric_cols <- sapply(data_df, is.numeric)
  
  # Exclude specific columns if they exist
  exclude_cols <- c("Specimen_Number", "Year", "Decade", "Month")
  numeric_cols[names(data_df) %in% exclude_cols] <- FALSE
  
  # Get numeric variables
  numeric_vars <- data_df[, numeric_cols, drop = FALSE]
  
  # Remove columns with too many NAs
  na_counts <- colSums(is.na(numeric_vars))
  numeric_vars <- numeric_vars[, na_counts < nrow(numeric_vars) * 0.5, drop = FALSE]
  
  if(ncol(numeric_vars) < 2) {
    cat("Warning: Not enough numeric variables for correlation analysis\n")
    cat("Numeric variables found:", paste(names(numeric_vars), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create correlation plot
  cor_plot <- chart.Correlation(numeric_vars, 
                               histogram = TRUE, 
                               pch = 19,
                               main = "Correlation Matrix of Morphological and Environmental Variables")
  
  # Save plot
  png("Figure_Correlation_Matrix.png", width = 10, height = 10, units = "in", res = 300)
  print(cor_plot)
  dev.off()
  
  cat("✓ Correlation matrix saved to: Figure_Correlation_Matrix.png\n")
  
  # Calculate specific correlations of interest
  cat("\nKey correlations:\n")
  
  if(all(c("Length_mm", "Width_mm") %in% names(data_df))) {
    cor_length_width <- cor.test(data_df$Length_mm, data_df$Width_mm, use = "complete.obs")
    cat("Length ~ Width: r =", round(cor_length_width$estimate, 4),
        "p =", format.pval(cor_length_width$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
  }
  
  if(all(c("Length_mm", "Latitude") %in% names(data_df))) {
    cor_length_lat <- cor.test(data_df$Length_mm, data_df$Latitude, use = "complete.obs")
    cat("Length ~ Latitude: r =", round(cor_length_lat$estimate, 4),
        "p =", format.pval(cor_length_lat$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
  }
  
  if(all(c("Length_mm", "Globosity_WoverL") %in% names(data_df))) {
    cor_length_shape <- cor.test(data_df$Length_mm, data_df$Globosity_WoverL, use = "complete.obs")
    cat("Length ~ Globosity: r =", round(cor_length_shape$estimate, 4),
        "p =", format.pval(cor_length_shape$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
  }
  
  return(cor_plot)
}

# ==============================================================================
# 4. MAIN ANALYSIS PIPELINE
# ==============================================================================

run_analysis_pipeline <- function() {
  
  cat(paste0(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""), "\n")
  cat("CALIFORNICONUS CALIFORNICUS MORPHOLOGY ANALYSIS\n")
  cat("Bergmann's Rule across Pleistocene, Holocene, and Anthropocene\n")
  cat(paste0(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""), "\n\n")
  
  # 1. Load and clean data
  data_list <- load_and_clean_data()
  Clean <- data_list$Clean
  
  cat("\n=== DATASET SUMMARY ===\n")
  cat("Total specimens:", nrow(Clean), "\n")
  
  if("Epoch" %in% names(Clean)) {
    cat("\nBy epoch:\n")
    epoch_table <- table(Clean$Epoch)
    print(epoch_table)
  }
  
  if("Data_Type" %in% names(Clean)) {
    cat("\nBy data type:\n")
    data_type_table <- table(Clean$Data_Type)
    print(data_type_table)
  }
  
  # 2. Create maps
  cat("\n=== CREATING VISUALIZATIONS ===\n")
  maps <- create_simple_maps(Clean)
  
  # 3. Test assumptions
  assumptions <- test_assumptions(Clean)
  
  # 4. Correlation analysis
  cor_plot <- create_correlation_analysis(Clean)
  
  # 5. Bergmann's Rule analysis
  bergmann_results <- analyze_bergmanns_rule(Clean)
  
  # 6. Generate summary statistics
  cat("\n=== GENERATING SUMMARY STATISTICS ===\n")
  
  if(inherits(Clean, "sf")) {
    Clean_df <- as.data.frame(Clean)
    # Remove geometry column if it exists
    if("geometry" %in% names(Clean_df)) {
      Clean_df$geometry <- NULL
    }
  } else {
    Clean_df <- Clean
  }
  
  # Create summary by epoch
  if("Epoch" %in% names(Clean_df) && "Length_mm" %in% names(Clean_df)) {
    summary_stats <- data.frame()
    
    for(epoch in levels(Clean_df$Epoch)) {
      epoch_data <- Clean_df %>% filter(Epoch == epoch)
      
      if(nrow(epoch_data) > 0) {
        epoch_summary <- data.frame(
          Epoch = epoch,
          N = nrow(epoch_data),
          Mean_Length = round(mean(epoch_data$Length_mm, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
          SD_Length = round(sd(epoch_data$Length_mm, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
          Min_Length = round(min(epoch_data$Length_mm, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
          Max_Length = round(max(epoch_data$Length_mm, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
        summary_stats <- rbind(summary_stats, epoch_summary)
      }
    }
    
    cat("\nSummary statistics by epoch:\n")
    print(summary_stats)
    
    write.csv(summary_stats, "Summary_Statistics.csv", row.names = FALSE)
    cat("\n✓ Summary statistics saved to: Summary_Statistics.csv\n")
  }
  
  # 7. Create model comparison table
  cat("\n=== CREATING MODEL COMPARISON ===\n")
  
  if(!is.null(bergmann_results)) {
    
    # Get sample sizes
    n_pleistocene <- sum(Clean_df$Epoch == "Pleistocene", na.rm = TRUE)
    n_holocene <- sum(Clean_df$Epoch == "Holocene", na.rm = TRUE)
    n_anthropocene <- sum(Clean_df$Epoch == "Anthropocene", na.rm = TRUE)
    
    model_comparison <- data.frame(
      Model = c("Length ~ Latitude (Overall)", 
                "Length ~ Latitude * Epoch",
                "Length ~ Latitude (Pleistocene)",
                "Length ~ Latitude (Holocene)",
                "Length ~ Latitude (Anthropocene)"),
      n = c(
        nrow(Clean_df),
        nrow(Clean_df),
        n_pleistocene,
        n_holocene,
        n_anthropocene
      ),
      Slope = c(
        round(coef(bergmann_results$models$overall)[2], 4),
        NA,
        if("Pleistocene" %in% names(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific)) 
          round(coef(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific$Pleistocene)[2], 4) else NA,
        if("Holocene" %in% names(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific)) 
          round(coef(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific$Holocene)[2], 4) else NA,
        if("Anthropocene" %in% names(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific)) 
          round(coef(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific$Anthropocene)[2], 4) else NA
      ),
      R_squared = c(
        round(summary(bergmann_results$models$overall)$r.squared, 4),
        round(summary(bergmann_results$models$epoch_interaction)$r.squared, 4),
        if("Pleistocene" %in% names(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific)) 
          round(summary(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific$Pleistocene)$r.squared, 4) else NA,
        if("Holocene" %in% names(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific)) 
          round(summary(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific$Holocene)$r.squared, 4) else NA,
        if("Anthropocene" %in% names(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific)) 
          round(summary(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific$Anthropocene)$r.squared, 4) else NA
      ),
      P_value = c(
        format.pval(summary(bergmann_results$models$overall)$coefficients[2, 4], digits = 3),
        if(!is.null(anova(bergmann_results$models$epoch_interaction))) 
          format.pval(anova(bergmann_results$models$epoch_interaction)$`Pr(>F)`[1], digits = 3) else NA,
        if("Pleistocene" %in% names(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific)) 
          format.pval(summary(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific$Pleistocene)$coefficients[2, 4], digits = 3) else NA,
        if("Holocene" %in% names(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific)) 
          format.pval(summary(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific$Holocene)$coefficients[2, 4], digits = 3) else NA,
        if("Anthropocene" %in% names(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific)) 
          format.pval(summary(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific$Anthropocene)$coefficients[2, 4], digits = 3) else NA
      ),
      Supports_Bergmann = c(
        ifelse(summary(bergmann_results$models$overall)$coefficients[2, 4] < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
        if(!is.null(anova(bergmann_results$models$epoch_interaction))) 
          ifelse(anova(bergmann_results$models$epoch_interaction)$`Pr(>F)`[3] < 0.05, "Varies", "Consistent") else NA,
        if("Pleistocene" %in% names(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific)) 
          ifelse(summary(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific$Pleistocene)$coefficients[2, 4] < 0.05, "Yes", "No") else NA,
        if("Holocene" %in% names(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific)) 
          ifelse(summary(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific$Holocene)$coefficients[2, 4] < 0.05, "Yes", "No") else NA,
        if("Anthropocene" %in% names(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific)) 
          ifelse(summary(bergmann_results$models$epoch_specific$Anthropocene)$coefficients[2, 4] < 0.05, "Yes", "No") else NA
      )
    )
    
    write.csv(model_comparison, "Model_Comparison.csv", row.names = FALSE)
    cat("\n✓ Model comparison saved to: Model_Comparison.csv\n")
    
    # Print key findings
    cat("\n=== KEY FINDINGS ===\n")
    
    overall_slope <- coef(bergmann_results$models$overall)[2]
    overall_p <- summary(bergmann_results$models$overall)$coefficients[2, 4]
    
    if(overall_p < 0.05) {
      cat(paste0("\n✓ BERGMANN'S RULE SUPPORTED: Shell length increases with latitude\n",
                 "  Slope: ", round(overall_slope, 4), " mm per degree latitude\n",
                 "  R²: ", round(summary(bergmann_results$models$overall)$r.squared, 3), "\n",
                 "  p-value: ", format.pval(overall_p, digits = 3), "\n"))
    } else {
      cat("\n✗ BERGMANN'S RULE NOT SUPPORTED: No significant relationship with latitude\n")
    }
    
    # Check epoch differences
    if(!is.null(anova(bergmann_results$models$epoch_interaction))) {
      epoch_p <- anova(bergmann_results$models$epoch_interaction)$`Pr(>F)`[3]
      if(!is.na(epoch_p) && epoch_p < 0.05) {
        cat("\n✓ EPOCH EFFECT: Relationship varies significantly between epochs\n")
      } else if(!is.na(epoch_p)) {
        cat("\n✗ NO EPOCH EFFECT: Relationship consistent across epochs\n")
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Return results
  results <- list(
    data = Clean,
    assumptions = assumptions,
    bergmann = bergmann_results,
    maps = maps,
    correlation = cor_plot
  )
  
  # Save results
  saveRDS(results, "Analysis_Results.rds")
  cat("\n✓ Complete analysis results saved to: Analysis_Results.rds\n")
  
  return(results)
}

# ==============================================================================
# 5. EXECUTE ANALYSIS
# ==============================================================================

# Run the analysis
cat("\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("STARTING ANALYSIS\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

tryCatch({
  results <- run_analysis_pipeline()
  
  cat("\n")
  cat(paste0(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""), "\n")
  cat("ANALYSIS COMPLETE!\n")
  cat(paste0(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""), "\n\n")
  
  cat("Output files generated:\n")
  cat("1. Data_Clean.csv - Cleaned dataset\n")
  cat("2. Figure1_Collection_Maps.png - Collection location maps\n")
  cat("3. Figure_Diagnostics.png - Diagnostic plots\n")
  cat("4. Figure_Correlation_Matrix.png - Correlation matrix\n")
  cat("5. Figure_Bergmanns_Rule.png - Bergmann's Rule visualization\n")
  cat("6. Summary_Statistics.csv - Summary statistics\n")
  cat("7. Model_Comparison.csv - Model comparison table\n")
  cat("8. Analysis_Results.rds - Complete analysis results\n\n")
  
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("\n")
  cat(paste0(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""), "\n")
  cat("ANALYSIS FAILED WITH ERROR:\n")
  cat(e$message, "\n")
  cat(paste0(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""), "\n\n")
  
  # Try to load pre-existing data files
  cat("Attempting to load pre-existing data files...\n")
  
  if(file.exists("Data_Clean.csv")) {
    cat("Loading Data_Clean.csv...\n")
    Clean <- read.csv("Data_Clean.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    
    # Run basic analyses on existing data
    cat("Running basic analyses...\n")
    
    # Create simple correlation plot (compatible way)
    numeric_cols <- sapply(Clean, is.numeric)
    numeric_vars <- Clean[, numeric_cols, drop = FALSE]
    
    if(ncol(numeric_vars) >= 2) {
      png("Figure_Correlation_Basic.png", width = 8, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
      chart.Correlation(numeric_vars, histogram = TRUE, pch = 19)
      dev.off()
      cat("Basic correlation plot saved to: Figure_Correlation_Basic.png\n")
    }
    
    # Check for Bergmann's Rule
    if(all(c("Length_mm", "Latitude") %in% names(Clean))) {
      lm_result <- lm(Length_mm ~ Latitude, data = Clean)
      cat("\nBasic Bergmann's Rule analysis:\n")
      cat("Slope:", round(coef(lm_result)[2], 4), "\n")
      cat("R²:", round(summary(lm_result)$r.squared, 4), "\n")
      cat("p-value:", format.pval(summary(lm_result)$coefficients[2, 4], digits = 3), "\n")
    }
  } else {
    cat("No existing data files found.\n")
  }
})

# Save workspace
if(exists("results")) {
  save.image("C_californicus_Analysis_Workspace.RData")
  cat("Workspace saved to: C_californicus_Analysis_Workspace.RData\n")
} else {
  cat("No results to save.\n")
}

# Print session info
cat("\n=== SESSION INFORMATION ===\n")
print(sessionInfo())

```


Prior
Data Clean-up        Post-processing: Raw -> Clean + SoCal       
*Fig 1a-b            Map of C. californicus range                
*Fig 2b              Shell allometry (Fig 2a of shell manual)             
*Fig 3a-d,Supp Fig 1 Size or Shape and Lat / Temp, Bergmann's    
*Fig 4a-f,Supp Fig 2 Size or Shape and Ecoregion + Proxmity      
*Tables 1-2          Statistics breakdown (of all figures)      
*Supp Fig 3          Correlations to human population density    
*Cope's Rule         Size by location ~ Epoch                    
>>>>>>> main

Overview of data and post-processing
```{r}

###### Upload data ######
# Need package for downloading data from Github repo
## install.packages("RCurl")
library("RCurl") 
## packageVersion("RCurl") # v1.98.1.1 

Raw <- read.csv(text=getURL("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MichaelMalloy/C_californicus_Morphology/master/SupplementalTable2_Data-Raw.csv")) 

Key <- read.csv(text=getURL("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MichaelMalloy/C_californicus_Morphology/master/SupplementalTable1_Data-Key.csv")) 

# Ensure blanks are read as NAs
###### Upload data ######


###### Data structure ######
# Quick view of RawData
str(Raw) 

# Plots of shell morphology measurements as a point of reference for outliers 
morphos = c("Length_mm", "Width_mm", "Aperture_mm", "Thickness_mm", "Globosity_WoverL", "Geometric_Mean")

# Need package for correlation charts
## install.packages("PerformanceAnalytics")
library("PerformanceAnalytics") 
## packageVersion("PerformanceAnalytics") # v2.0.4

# Need package pipes ( %>% or "then" )
## install.packages("tidyverse")
library("tidyverse") 
## packageVersion("tidyverse") # v1.3.0

# Check for outliers by correlation matrix
Raw_CorrMatrix = chart.Correlation(Raw %>% 
                                     select(morphos)) 
Raw_CorrMatrix
###### Data structure ######


###### Clean-up ######
# Organization of locations in dataset
## Re-order counties, North to South
counties = c("Sonoma", "San Mateo", "Monterey", "San Luis Obispo", "Santa Barbara", "Ventura", "Los Angeles", "Orange", "San Diego", "Baja California")
Raw$County = factor(Raw$County, levels=counties) # order counties
## Order SoCal counties and locales for when subsetting SoCal data
socal = c("Santa Barbara", "Ventura", "Los Angeles", "Orange", "San Diego")
socal_locations = c("SB", "NLA", "LA", "SLA", "SD", "ANA", "SCZI", "SRI", "SMI", "CAT", "SBI", "SNI", "SCI") # socal location order
la = "Los Angeles"

# Filter outliers (min/max values identified by Raw correlations)
## Subset for including back in as part of Clean / filtered set (below)
No_TA = subset(Raw, is.na(Raw$Thickness_mm)) # 775 no T or A
Raw_iNat = subset(Raw, Raw$Collection=="iNat") # 99 from iNat
## Confirmed to be operator error (measuring or datasheet input)
OutliersRemoved = Raw %>% # 2996 obs 
  filter(Thickness_mm < 2) %>% # low T
  filter(!(Width_mm < 15 & Thickness_mm > 2)) %>% # too thick for W
  filter(Aperture_mm < 6) %>%  # small A
  filter(!(Length_mm > 20 & Aperture_mm <2)) %>% # A too small for L
  rbind(No_TA) %>%  # adds 775 shells back in 
  filter(Width_mm > 2) %>%  # too narrow (note this gets rid of iNat)
  rbind(Raw_iNat) %>% # add iNat back in
  filter(Globosity_WoverL > 0.42 & Globosity_WoverL < 0.71 ) %>% 
  data.frame # ensure it's a data frame
## Remove 25 shells from Pliocene (too old, few)
OutliersRemoved=subset(OutliersRemoved, !Epoch=="Pliocene")
## Filter specimens that may skew in being in the open ocean and/or too north or south
min(OutliersRemoved$Latitude) # 26.43
max(OutliersRemoved$Latitude) # 38.16
### Ensure updated Clean dataset has 3 or more specimens per lot
Clean = OutliersRemoved %>% 
  filter(Latitude > 26.43) %>% 
  filter(Latitude < 38.16) %>% 
  group_by(Lot_Number) %>% 
  mutate(Specimens_per_Lot = length((Lot_Number))) %>%
  ungroup %>% 
  filter(Specimens_per_Lot > 2 | Collection == "iNat") # keep catalogs with > 2 in lot and iNat (1 per 'lot')
## Validation check for NA values for final data set
cat("\n\n",
    sum(is.na(Clean$Globosity_WoverL)), "have no globosity ratio,\n", # 0
    sum(is.na(Clean$Geometric_Mean)), "have no geometric mean,\n", # 99 [iNat]
    sum(is.na(Clean$Width_mm)), "have no width measurement,\n", # 99 [iNat]
    sum(is.na(Clean$Length_mm)), "have no length measurement,\n", # 99 [iNat]
    sum(is.na(Clean$Thickness_mm)), "have no thickness measurement,\n", # 742 = 99 [iNat] + 338 PR + 273 Pleisto
    sum(is.na(Clean$Aperture_mm)), "have no aperture measurement" ) # 742 = 99 [iNat] + 338 PR + 273 Pleisto
## Order the data
Clean %>% 
  group_by(Lot_Number) %>% 
  summarise(N = length(Specimen_Number))
Clean %>% 
  group_by(Epoch) %>% 
  summarise(N = length(Specimen_Number)) 
## Re-do of correlation chart
Clean_CorrMatrix = chart.Correlation(Clean %>% 
                                       select(morphos)) # much improved
Clean_CorrMatrix
dev.copy(png,'Figure2b_Clean.jpg')
dev.off()

# Subset Clean (2908 of 2996 from Raw) to SoCal (2608)
SoCal = Clean %>% 
  filter(County %in% 
           socal) 
SoCal$Location = factor(SoCal$Location, levels=socal_locations) # order 
# Distribution of shells by county *most are in LA (1372)
SoCal %>% 
  group_by(County) %>% 
  summarise(N = length(Epoch)) %>% 
  data.frame 
# Check correlation for SoCal subset
SoCal_CorrMatrix = chart.Correlation(SoCal %>% 
                                       select(morphos)) # Also looks good
SoCal_CorrMatrix
dev.copy(png,'Figure2b_SoCal.jpg')
dev.off()

# Subset 
LA = SoCal %>% 
  filter(County %in% 
           la) 
LA$Location = factor(LA$Location, levels=socal_locations) # order 
# Check correlation for LA subset
LA_CorrMatrix = chart.Correlation(LA %>%
                                    select(morphos)) #
LA_CorrMatrix
dev.copy(png,'Figure2b_LA.jpg')
dev.off()
###### Clean-up ######



###### Save cleaned-up data for downstream visualization and analysis ######
# Data structure for Clean and SoCal subset
sort(unique(Clean$Collection)) # Unique collections: 9
sort(unique(Clean$Lot_Number)) # Unique lots: 382 
sort(unique(Clean$County)) # Unique counties: 9
sort(unique(Clean$Location)) # Unique locations: 19
sort(unique(Clean$Coastal_Proximity)) # Unique location types: 2
sort(unique(Clean$Habitat)) # Unique habitats: 4
sort(unique(Clean$Depth)) # Unique depths: 3

sort(unique(SoCal$Collection)) # Unique collections: 9
sort(unique(SoCal$Lot_Number)) # Unique lots: 339
sort(unique(SoCal$County)) # Unique counties: 4
sort(unique(SoCal$Location)) # Unique locations: 13

write.csv(Clean, file = "SupplementalTable3_Data-Clean.csv")
write.csv(SoCal, file = "SupplementalTable4_Data-SoCal.csv") 

# Validation check 
Clean <- read.csv(text=getURL("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MichaelMalloy/C_californicus_Morphology/master/SupplementalTable3_Data-Clean.csv")) 
SoCal <- read.csv(text=getURL("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MichaelMalloy/C_californicus_Morphology/master/SupplementalTable4_Data-SoCal.csv")) 


###### Save cleaned-up data for downstream visualization and analysis ######

# Subset data by Epoch to color code points
Anthro = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Anthropocene") # 1492
<<<<<<< HEAD
Holo = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Holocene") # 1138
Pleisto = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Pleistocene") # 305

=======
Holo = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Holocene") # 1106
Pleisto = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Pleistocene") # 305

Anthro_SoCal = subset(SoCal, Epoch=="Anthropocene") # 1366
Holo_SoCal = subset(SoCal, Epoch=="Holocene") # 1020
Pleisto_SoCal = subset(SoCal, Epoch=="Pleistocene") # 211

>>>>>>> main
```

Figure 1a-b: Maps of shell locations (Clean and SoCal Data) 
```{r}

# Need packages for map coordinates
## if(!requireNamespace("devtools")) install.packages("devtools")
## devtools::install_github("dkahle/ggmap")
library("ggmap")
## packageVersion("ggmap") # v3.0.0
## install.packages("rgdal")
library("rgdal")
## packageVersion("rgdal") # v1.4.8
# Need package for plots of maps
## install.packages("ggplot2")
library("ggplot2")
## packageVersion("ggplot2") # v3.2.1
# Need package for gridding maps as one figure
## install.packages("cowplot")
library("cowplot")
## packageVersion("cowplot") # v1.0.0

# This code is personalized by account and only runs if you have a key
## Instructions: https://github.com/dkahle/ggmap
## Visit your personal Google Maps Platform to retrieve key if you have one
## ggmap::register_google(key = " ") 

# Load polygons for ecoregions and call them after downloaded in your folder 
<<<<<<< HEAD
# shape files from http://www.marineregions.org/ (also in repo)
=======
## shape files from http://www.marineregions.org/ (also in repo)
>>>>>>> main
NC_poly=readOGR( "northern_ecoregions" , layer="ecoregions") 
SCB_poly=readOGR( "southern_ecoregions" , layer="ecoregions") 
MT_poly=readOGR( "magdaelena_ecoregions" , layer="ecoregions") 

# Figure 1a
<<<<<<< HEAD
# Parameters for All points
All_bbox <- make_bbox(lat = Latitude, lon = Longitude, data = Clean)
All_bbox
#       left     bottom      right        top 
#   -122.9015   25.8435 -114.7285   38.7465 

All=get_map(location=c(-125,20,-105,40), # adjust to include regions
            zoom = 5, 
            maptype = "terrain") 

# Create box for zoom of SoCal
SoCal_box = data.frame(x=c(-121,-121,-116.5,-116.5,-121), 
       y=c(35,32,32,35,35)) 

# Plot map
=======
## Parameters for All points
All_bbox <- make_bbox(lat = Latitude, lon = Longitude, data = Clean)
All_bbox
##     left        bottom    right       top 
##     -122.786    27.359    -114.734    37.721 
All=get_map(location=c(-125,20,-105,40), # adjust to include regions
            zoom = 5, 
            maptype = "terrain") 
## Create box for zoom of SoCal
SoCal_box = data.frame(x=c(-121,-121,-116.5,-116.5,-121), 
       y=c(35,32,32,35,35)) 
## Plot map
>>>>>>> main
Clean_map = ggmap(All) +
  geom_polygon(data = NC_poly, aes(x = long, y = lat),
               fill = "white", alpha = 0.5) + # white
  geom_polygon(data = SCB_poly, aes(x = long, y = lat),
               fill = "blue", alpha = 0.3) + # blue
  geom_polygon(data = MT_poly, aes(x = long, y = lat),
               fill = "#666666", alpha = 0.5) + # grey
  stat_density_2d(data = Clean, # density
                  aes(x = Longitude,
                      y = Latitude,
                      fill = stat(level)),
                  alpha = .25,
                  bins = 250,
                  geom = "polygon") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = "black") + # color fill, black
  geom_point(data = Anthro, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude), 
             color = "#FC4E07", # orange
             alpha = 0.15,
             size = 1) + 
  geom_point(data = Holo, aes(x = Longitude,y = Latitude), 
             color = "#E7B800", # gold
             alpha = 0.15,
             size = 1) + 
  geom_point(data = Pleisto, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude), 
             color = "#00AFBB", # blue
             alpha = 0.15,
             size = 1) + 
  geom_point(data = Key, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude), # asbsent data
             color = "#FC4E07", # orange
             shape = 4,
<<<<<<< HEAD
             size = 1.5) + # manually added Punta Abreojos white circle
=======
             size = 1.5) + # manually add Punta Abreojos white circle
>>>>>>> main
  geom_path(data=SoCal_box, aes(x,y), color="black", lwd=1) + # insert box
  labs(x="Longitude", y="Latitude") 
Clean_map

# Figure 1b
SoCal_bbox = make_bbox(lat = Latitude, lon = Longitude, data = SoCal)
SoCal_bbox
##    left        bottom    right       top
##    -120.6685   32.4635 -116.9615   34.5865

            zoom = 7, 
            maptype = "satellite") 

<<<<<<< HEAD
CleanSoCal_map = ggmap(SoCal) +
  stat_density_2d(data = Clean_SoCal, # density
=======

SoCal$Longitude = as.numeric(SoCal$Longitude)

SoCal = data.frame(SoCal)

SoCal_map = ggmap(SoCal) +
  stat_density_2d(data = SoCal, # density
>>>>>>> main
                  aes(x = Longitude,
                      y = Latitude,
                      fill = stat(level)),
                  alpha = .35,
                  bins = 250,
                  geom = "polygon") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = "black") + 
  geom_point(data = Anthro_SoCal, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude), 
             color = "#FC4E07",
             alpha = 0.15,
             size = 2) + 
  geom_point(data = Holo_SoCal, aes(x = Longitude,y = Latitude), 
             color = "#E7B800", 
             alpha = 0.15,
             size = 2) + 
  geom_point(data = Pleisto_SoCal, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude), 
             color = "#00AFBB",
             alpha = 0.15,
             size = 2) + 
    geom_path(data=SoCal_box, aes(x,y), color="black", lwd=1) + # insert box
  labs(x="Longitude", y="Latitude")
SoCal_map


ggsave("Figure1b_Data-Clean-SoCal_Map.png", dpi = 800)


ggsave("Figure1a_Data-Clean_Map.png", dpi = 800)

##### Create Map (Figure 1a-b) #####
#  Need a personalized account with API key (map key) for Google maps
## Instructions: https://github.com/dkahle/ggmap
## ggmap::register_google(key = " ") 
### More helpful info: https://www.littlemissdata.com/blog/maps 

# Figure 1a: Larger map
## Parameters for All points
All_bbox <- make_bbox(lat = Latitude, lon = Longitude, data = df)
All_bbox # Informs us what to set for parameters
##     left        bottom    right       top 
##     -122.786    27.359    -114.734    37.721 
All=get_map(location=c(-129,20,-105,42), # adjust to include regions
            zoom = 5, 
            maptype = "terrain") # can adjust this type as well
## Create box for zoom of SoCal
SoCal_box = data.frame(x=c(-121,-121,-116.5,-116.5,-121), 
       y=c(35,32,32,35,35)) 
## Plot map 
Figure1a = ggmap(All) + # Google map of parameters
  geom_polygon(data = NC_poly, aes(x = long, y = lat), # polygons (shape files)
               fill = "white", alpha = 0.5) + 
  geom_polygon(data = SCB_poly, aes(x = long, y = lat),
               fill = "blue", alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_polygon(data = MT_poly, aes(x = long, y = lat),
               fill = "#666666", alpha = 0.5) + # grey
  stat_density_2d(data = df, # hypothetical population density
                  aes(x = Longitude,
                      y = Latitude,
                      fill = stat(level)),
                  alpha = .25,
                  bins = 250, # can adjust this for resolution
                  geom = "polygon") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = "black") + # fills density layers as black 
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) + # colors by Epoch
  geom_point(data = df, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, color = Epoch), 
             alpha = 0.15,
             size = 1) + # layers points
  geom_path(data=SoCal_box, aes(x,y), color="black", lwd=1) + # insert box
  labs(x="Longitude", y="Latitude") +
  theme(legend.position="none")
Figure1a

# Figure 1b: Zoom-in of SoCal
SoCal=get_map(location=c(-121,32,-116.5,35), # adjust based on SoCal_box
            maptype = "terrain-background") # can adjust this type as well
## Plot map
Figure1b = ggmap(SoCal) +
  geom_polygon(data = NC_poly, aes(x = long, y = lat),
               fill = "white", alpha = 0.5) + # white
  geom_polygon(data = SCB_poly, aes(x = long, y = lat),
               fill = "blue", alpha = 0.3) + # blue
    stat_density_2d(data = df, # density
                  aes(x = Longitude,
                      y = Latitude,
                      fill = stat(level)),
                  alpha = .25,
                  bins = 250,
                  geom = "polygon") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = "black") + # color fill, black
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  geom_point(data = df, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, color = Epoch), 
             alpha = 0.15,
             size = 1) +
  theme(axis.title.x=element_blank(), axis.title.y=element_blank())
Figure1b # Note the ecoregions cut-out if polygon not fully included

# Grid using cowplot, and save
Figure1 = plot_grid(Figure1a, Figure1b, labels = c("A", "B"), label_size=12)
Figure1   

ggsave("Figure1.png", dpi = 800) 

##### Create Map (Figure 1a-b) #####

```

<<<<<<< HEAD
Q1) Are shell size and shape independent or dependent metrics of each other? Independent
Figure 2b: Allometry - shell shape vs size (Clean_New) 
=======
Figure 2b: Are shell size, mass, or shape independent or dependent metrics of each other?
>>>>>>> main
```{r}

# Filter out set of specimens more in the open ocean and south of the rest + most northern
min(Clean$Latitude) # 26.43
max(Clean$Latitude) # 38.16
Clean_New = Clean %>% 
  filter(Latitude > 26.43) %>% # 2935 - 2902 = 33 obs removed
  filter(Latitude < 38.16) # 2902 - 2897 = 5 obs removed

# install.packages("GGally")
library(GGally)
# packageVersion("GGally") # v1.4.0

# Create a function for including linear regression line
my_fn <- function(data, mapping, ...){
  p <- ggplot(data = data, mapping = mapping) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_smooth(method=lm, color="black", ...) 
  p
}

# Figure 2b
<<<<<<< HEAD
Fig2b = Clean_New[,c("Epoch", "Length_mm", "Width_mm", "Globosity_WoverL")] 
# Rename columns
names(Fig2b) = c("Epoch", "Length", "Width", "Globosity") 
=======
Fig2b = Clean[,c("Epoch", "Length_mm", "Width_mm", "Globosity_WoverL", "Geometric_Mean")] 
# Rename columns
names(Fig2b) = c("Epoch", "Length (size)", "Width (size)", "Globosity (shape)", "geomM (mass)") 
>>>>>>> main
# Create figure (automatically excludes NAs)
Fig2b_chart = ggpairs(Fig2b, aes(color = Epoch, alpha = 0.85), lower = list(continuous = my_fn), upper = list(continuous = wrap("cor", size = 4.25)))
# Change colors manually with a loop through each plot
for(i in 1:Fig2b_chart$nrow) {
  for(j in 1:Fig2b_chart$ncol){
    Fig2b_chart[i,j] <- Fig2b_chart[i,j] + 
        scale_fill_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
        scale_color_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB"))  
  }
}

Fig2b_chart + 
  theme_classic() # theme for figure
ggsave("Figure2b_Data-Clean-New_Shell-Allometry.png", dpi = 800)
<<<<<<< HEAD

# Statistics
summary(lm(Length ~ Width, data = Fig2b)) # Radj = 0.9534, p < 2.2e-16
summary(lm(Length ~ Globosity, data = Fig2b)) # Radj = 0.08497, p < 2.2e-16
summary(lm(Width ~ Globosity, data = Fig2b)) # Radj = 0.007467, p = 2.854e-06  

# Length = Width, globosity independent of length and width
## shape = globosity
## size = length=width (better to go with width as size over length given lm results)
### Though, barely a difference in the larger scope of Radj (for L ~ g vs W ~ g)
=======

# Statistics
names(Fig2b) = c("Epoch", "Length", "Width", "Globosity", "geomM") 
summary(lm(Length ~ Width, data = Fig2b)) # Radj = 0.9534, p < 2.2e-16
summary(lm(Length ~ Globosity, data = Fig2b)) # Radj = 0.0853, p < 2.2e-16
summary(lm(Width ~ Globosity, data = Fig2b)) # Radj = 0.007822, p = 2.796e-06 
summary(lm(Length ~ geomM, data = Fig2b)) # Radj = 0.9884, p < 2.2e-16
summary(lm(Width ~ geomM, data = Fig2b)) # Radj = 0.988, p < 2.2e-16
summary(lm(Globosity ~ geomM, data = Fig2b)) # Radj = 0.03698, p < 2.2e-16

# Length = Width, dependent on each other and synonymous with size
# Globosity (shape) is an independent metric of size 
## Width slightly more independent (horizontal line to globosity) than Length
### Thus, use Width as proxy for size 
# geomM (mass) is synonymous with size in this instance, and independent of shape
## Do we want to then only use geomM (mass) and globosity (shape)?
### Note Width is still the most horizontal of the 3 (Length, Width, geomM) for Fig 2b ()
>>>>>>> main

```

Q2) Do size/shape vary by epoch in relation to latitude or temperature? Yes.
<<<<<<< HEAD
Figure 3a-d, Supp Figure 1: Comparison of size and shape for Latitude vs SST (Clean_New)
=======
Figure 3a-d, Supp Figure 1: Comparison of size and shape for Latitude vs SST (Clean)
>>>>>>> main
```{r}

# Regression Plot function for y = mx + b
ggplotRegression <- function (fit) {

require(ggplot2)

ggplot(fit$model, aes_string(x = names(fit$model)[2], y = names(fit$model)[1])) + 
  geom_point() +
  stat_smooth(method = "lm", col = "red") +
  labs(title = paste("Adj R2 = ",signif(summary(fit)$adj.r.squared, 5),
                     "Intercept =",signif(fit$coef[[1]],5 ),
                     " Slope =",signif(fit$coef[[2]], 5),
                     " P =",signif(summary(fit)$coef[2,4], 5)))
}

# Supplemental Figure 1: What is the relationship between latitude and SST?
<<<<<<< HEAD
SuppFig1 = ggplot(data = Clean_New, 
=======
SuppFig1 = ggplot(data = Clean, 
>>>>>>> main
                      aes(x = Latitude, y = Mean_SST,
                      color = Epoch,
                      alpha = 0.4)) +
  annotate("rect",ymin=-Inf,ymax=Inf,xmin=32.56,xmax=34.49, # adjust by transformation
            alpha=0.2, fill="black") + # shade square
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = lm) +
  geom_smooth(method = lm, color = "black") + # 95% confidence
  geom_point(aes(color = Epoch)) + 
  geom_errorbar(stat="summary", fun.data="mean_se", fun.args = list(mult = 1.96), width=0) +
    geom_smooth(aes(color = Epoch, fill = Epoch), method=lm, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.35) + # 95% confidence interval
  labs(x="Latitude", y="Mean SST")
SuppFig1 +
  theme_classic() 
ggsave("SuppFig1_Data-Clean-New_Latitude-MeanSST.png", dpi = 800)
# Regression by Epoch
<<<<<<< HEAD
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Latitude, data = Clean_New))
# ** y = 38.222 - 0.660x, r^2 = 0.157, p = 5.302 x 10^-110
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Latitude, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Anthropocene")))
# ** y = 43.679 - 0.810x, r^2 = 0.413, p = 1.487 x 10^-174
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Latitude, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Holocene")))
# ** y = 35.303 - 0.565x, r^2 = 0.259, p = 7.868 x 10^-74
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Latitude, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Pleistocene")))
# ** y = 32.702 - 0.591, r^2 = 0.457, p = 1.704 x 10^-42

# Figure 3a-d (size is represented as Width) 
Fig3a = ggplot(data = Clean_New, 
                      aes(x = Latitude, y = Width_mm,
=======
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Latitude, data = Clean))
# ** y = 38.222 - 0.660x, r^2 = 0.157, p = 5.302 x 10^-110
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Latitude, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Anthropocene")))
# ** y = 43.679 - 0.810x, r^2 = 0.413, p = 1.487 x 10^-174
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Latitude, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Holocene")))
# ** y = 35.303 - 0.565x, r^2 = 0.259, p = 7.868 x 10^-74
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Latitude, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Pleistocene")))
# ** y = 32.702 - 0.591, r^2 = 0.457, p = 1.704 x 10^-42

# Figure 3a-d (size is represented as Width) 
Fig3a = ggplot(data = Clean, 
                      aes(x = Latitude, y = Length_mm,
>>>>>>> main
                      color = Epoch,
                      alpha = 0.4)) +
  annotate("rect",ymin=-Inf,ymax=Inf,xmin=32.56,xmax=34.49, # adjust by transformation
            alpha=0.2, fill="black") + # shade square
<<<<<<< HEAD
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = lm) +
  geom_smooth(method = lm, color = "black") + # 95% confidence
  geom_point(aes(color = Epoch)) + 
  geom_errorbar(stat="summary", fun.data="mean_se", fun.args = list(mult = 1.96), width=0) +
    geom_smooth(aes(color = Epoch, fill = Epoch), method=lm, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.35) + # 95% confidence interval
  labs(x="Latitude", y="Size (Width mm)")
Fig3a +
  theme_classic() 
ggsave("Fig3a_Data-Clean_New_Latitude-Width.png", dpi = 800)
# Regression by Epoch
ggplotRegression(lm(Latitude ~ Width_mm, data = Clean_New))
# ** y = 32.837 + 0.057x, r^2 = 0.043, p = 1.056 x 10^-28
## Relationships by Epoch are not important based on global interactive model results (Tab 1)

Fig3b = ggplot(data = Clean_New, 
                      aes(x = Mean_SST, y = Width_mm,
                      color = Epoch,
                      alpha = 0.4)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = lm) +
  geom_smooth(method = lm, color = "black") + # 95% confidence
  geom_point(aes(color = Epoch)) + 
  geom_errorbar(stat="summary", fun.data="mean_se", fun.args = list(mult = 1.96), width=0) +
    geom_smooth(aes(color = Epoch, fill = Epoch), method=lm, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.35) + # 95% confidence interval
  labs(x="Mean SST", y="Size (Width mm)")
Fig3b +
  theme_classic() 
ggsave("Fig3b_Data-Clean_New_MeanSST-Width.png", dpi = 800)
# Regression by Epoch
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Width_mm, data = Clean_New))
# y = 16.050 + 0.005x, r^2 = 2.267 x 10^-4, p = 0.545
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Width_mm, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Anthropocene")))
# y = 16.663 - 7.955x10^-5x, r^2 = -0.001, p = 0.993
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Width_mm, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Holocene")))
# ** y = 17.009 - 0.065x, r^2 = 0.057, p = 7.759 x 10^-26
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Width_mm, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Pleistocene")))
# y = 13.191 - 0.021x, r^2 = 0.001, p = 0.255

Fig3c = ggplot(data = Clean_New, 
                      aes(x = Latitude, y = Globosity_WoverL,
                      color = Epoch,
                      alpha = 0.4)) +
  annotate("rect",ymin=-Inf,ymax=Inf,xmin=32.56,xmax=34.49, # adjust by transformation
            alpha=0.2, fill="black") + # shade square
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = lm) +
  geom_smooth(method = lm, color = "black") + # 95% confidence
  geom_point(aes(color = Epoch)) + 
  geom_errorbar(stat="summary", fun.data="mean_se", fun.args = list(mult = 1.96), width=0) +
    geom_smooth(aes(color = Epoch, fill = Epoch), method=lm, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.35) + # 95% confidence interval
  labs(x="Latitude", y="Shape (Globosity, W/L)")
Fig3c +
  theme_classic() 
ggsave("Fig3c_Data-Clean_New_Latitude-Globosity.png", dpi = 800)
# Regression by Epoch
ggplotRegression(lm(Latitude ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = Clean_New))
# ** y = 31.951 + 2.645x, r^2 = 0.013, p = 8.232e-10
ggplotRegression(lm(Latitude ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Anthropocene")))
# ** y = 31.235 + 3.662x, r^2 = 0.028, p = 5.554e-11
ggplotRegression(lm(Latitude ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Holocene")))
# y = 33.297 + 0.697x, r^2 = -1.745e-5, p = 0.322
ggplotRegression(lm(Latitude ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Pleistocene")))
# ** y = 36.22 - 5.014x, r^2 = 0.025, p = 0.004

Fig3d = ggplot(data = Clean_New, 
                      aes(x = Mean_SST, y = Globosity_WoverL,
                      color = Epoch,
                      alpha = 0.4)) +
=======
>>>>>>> main
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = lm) +
  geom_smooth(method = lm, color = "black") + # 95% confidence
  geom_point(aes(color = Epoch)) + 
  geom_errorbar(stat="summary", fun.data="mean_se", fun.args = list(mult = 1.96), width=0) +
    geom_smooth(aes(color = Epoch, fill = Epoch), method=lm, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.35) + # 95% confidence interval
<<<<<<< HEAD
  labs(x="Mean SST", y="Shape (Globosity, W/L)")
Fig3d +
  theme_classic() 
ggsave("Fig3d_Data-Clean_New_MeanSST-Globosity.png", dpi = 800)
# Regression by Epoch
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = Clean_New))
# ** y = 17.072 - 1.619x, r^2 = 0.001, p = 0.024
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Anthropocene")))
# ** y = 21.187 - 7.901x, r^2 = 0.083, p = 4.988e-30
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Holocene")))
# ** y = 13.424 + 4.866x, r^2 = 0.035, p = 3.155e-10
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Pleistocene")))
# ** y = 13.21 - 0.237x, r^2 = -0.003, p = 0.875

ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Coastal_Proximity, data = subset(Clean_New))) # p = 0.923
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Coastal_Proximity, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Anthropocene"))) # p < 0.0001
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Coastal_Proximity, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Holocene")))
# p < 0.0001
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Coastal_Proximity, data = subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Pleistocene"))) # p = 0.03
=======
  labs(x="Latitude", y="Size (Length mm)") +
  theme(legend.position = "none") 
# Regression by Epoch
ggplotRegression(lm(Latitude ~ Length_mm, data = Clean))
# ** y = 32.837 + 0.057x, r^2 = 0.043, p = 1.056 x 10^-28
## Relationships by Epoch are not important based on global interactive model results (Tab 1)

Fig3b = ggplot(data = Clean, 
                      aes(x = Mean_SST, y = Length_mm,
                      color = Epoch,
                      alpha = 0.4)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = lm) +
  geom_smooth(method = lm, color = "black") + # 95% confidence
  geom_point(aes(color = Epoch)) + 
  geom_errorbar(stat="summary", fun.data="mean_se", fun.args = list(mult = 1.96), width=0) +
    geom_smooth(aes(color = Epoch, fill = Epoch), method=lm, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.35) + # 95% confidence interval
  labs(x="Mean SST", y="") +
  theme(legend.position = "none") 
# Regression by Epoch
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Length_mm, data = Clean))
# y = 16.050 + 0.005x, r^2 = 2.267 x 10^-4, p = 0.545
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Length_mm, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Anthropocene")))
# y = 16.663 - 7.955x10^-5x, r^2 = -0.001, p = 0.993
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Length_mm, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Holocene")))
# ** y = 17.009 - 0.065x, r^2 = 0.057, p = 7.759 x 10^-26
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Length_mm, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Pleistocene")))
# y = 13.191 - 0.021x, r^2 = 0.001, p = 0.255

Figure3 = plot_grid(Fig3a , Fig3b, labels = c("A", "B"), label_size=12)
ggsave("Fig3_Length.png", dpi = 800)








Fig3c = ggplot(data = Clean, 
                      aes(x = Latitude, y = Globosity_WoverL,
                      color = Epoch,
                      alpha = 0.4)) +
  annotate("rect",ymin=-Inf,ymax=Inf,xmin=32.56,xmax=34.49, # adjust by transformation
            alpha=0.2, fill="black") + # shade square
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = lm) +
  geom_smooth(method = lm, color = "black") + # 95% confidence
  geom_point(aes(color = Epoch)) + 
  geom_errorbar(stat="summary", fun.data="mean_se", fun.args = list(mult = 1.96), width=0) +
    geom_smooth(aes(color = Epoch, fill = Epoch), method=lm, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.35) + # 95% confidence interval
  labs(x="Latitude", y="Shape (Globosity, W/L)")
Fig3c +
  theme_classic() 
ggsave("Fig3c_Data-Clean_Latitude-Globosity.png", dpi = 800)
# Regression by Epoch
ggplotRegression(lm(Latitude ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = Clean))
# ** y = 31.951 + 2.645x, r^2 = 0.013, p = 8.232e-10
ggplotRegression(lm(Latitude ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Anthropocene")))
# ** y = 31.235 + 3.662x, r^2 = 0.028, p = 5.554e-11
ggplotRegression(lm(Latitude ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Holocene")))
# y = 33.297 + 0.697x, r^2 = -1.745e-5, p = 0.322
ggplotRegression(lm(Latitude ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Pleistocene")))
# ** y = 36.22 - 5.014x, r^2 = 0.025, p = 0.004

Fig3d = ggplot(data = Clean, 
                      aes(x = Mean_SST, y = Globosity_WoverL,
                      color = Epoch,
                      alpha = 0.4)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#FC4E07", "#E7B800", "#00AFBB")) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = lm) +
  geom_smooth(method = lm, color = "black") + # 95% confidence
  geom_point(aes(color = Epoch)) + 
  geom_errorbar(stat="summary", fun.data="mean_se", fun.args = list(mult = 1.96), width=0) +
    geom_smooth(aes(color = Epoch, fill = Epoch), method=lm, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.35) + # 95% confidence interval
  labs(x="Mean SST", y="Shape (Globosity, W/L)")
Fig3d +
  theme_classic() 
ggsave("Fig3d_Data-Clean_MeanSST-Globosity.png", dpi = 800)
# Regression by Epoch
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = Clean))
# ** y = 17.072 - 1.619x, r^2 = 0.001, p = 0.024
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Anthropocene")))
# ** y = 21.187 - 7.901x, r^2 = 0.083, p = 4.988e-30
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Holocene")))
# ** y = 13.424 + 4.866x, r^2 = 0.035, p = 3.155e-10
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Globosity_WoverL, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Pleistocene")))
# ** y = 13.21 - 0.237x, r^2 = -0.003, p = 0.875




ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Coastal_Proximity, data = subset(Clean))) # p = 0.923
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Coastal_Proximity, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Anthropocene"))) # p < 0.0001
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Coastal_Proximity, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Holocene")))
# p < 0.0001
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Coastal_Proximity, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Pleistocene"))) # p = 0.03


# Regression by Epoch
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Geometric_Mean, data = Clean))
# 
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Geometric_Mean, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Anthropocene")))
#
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Geometric_Mean, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Holocene")))
# **
ggplotRegression(lm(Mean_SST ~ Geometric_Mean, data = subset(Clean, Epoch=="Pleistocene")))
# 
>>>>>>> main

```

Q3) Do size/shape vary in relation to ecoregion and coastal proximity? Yes.
Figure 4a-f, Supp Fig 2: Size vs shape for ecoregion and coastal proximity
(Clean_New)
```{r}

# install.packages("ggplot2")
library(ggplot2)
# packageVersion("ggplot2") # v3.1.0

# Supplemental Figure 2: What is the relationship between ecoregion, coastal proximity, and SST?
min(Clean_New$Mean_SST) # 9.68
max(Clean_New$Mean_SST) # 19.52831

SuppFig2a <- ggplot(subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Pleistocene"), aes(x = Ecoregion, y = Mean_SST, fill = Coastal_Proximity)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha=0.7) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(9.5,20)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Mean SST") +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Ecoregion") +
  ggtitle("(a) Pleistocene") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, family = "Tahoma", face = "bold"),
        text = element_text(size = 12, family = "Tahoma"),
        axis.title = element_text(face="bold"),
        axis.text.x=element_text(size = 11)) 
SuppFig2a
ggsave("SuppFig2a_Data-Clean-New_Ecoregion-Proximity-MeanSST.png", dpi = 800)

SuppFig2b <- ggplot(subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Holocene"), aes(x = Ecoregion, y = Mean_SST, fill = Coastal_Proximity)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha=0.7) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(9.5,20)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Mean SST") +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Ecoregion") +
  ggtitle("(b) Holocene") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, family = "Tahoma", face = "bold"),
        text = element_text(size = 12, family = "Tahoma"),
        axis.title = element_text(face="bold"),
        axis.text.x=element_text(size = 11)) 
SuppFig2b
ggsave("SuppFig2b_Data-Clean-New_Ecoregion-Proximity-MeanSST.png", dpi = 800)

SuppFig2c <- ggplot(subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Anthropocene"), aes(x = Ecoregion, y = Mean_SST, fill = Coastal_Proximity)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha=0.7) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(9.5,20)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Mean SST") +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Ecoregion") +
  ggtitle("(c) Anthropocene") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, family = "Tahoma", face = "bold"),
        text = element_text(size = 12, family = "Tahoma"),
        axis.title = element_text(face="bold"),
        axis.text.x=element_text(size = 11)) 
SuppFig2c
ggsave("SuppFig2c_Data-Clean-New_Ecoregion-Proximity-MeanSST.png", dpi = 800)

# Figure 4a-f
min(Clean_New$Width_mm) # 3.23
max(Clean_New$Width_mm) # 23.65
min(Clean_New$Globosity_WoverL) # 0.43
max(Clean_New$Globosity_WoverL) # 0.7

<<<<<<< HEAD
Fig4a <- ggplot(subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Pleistocene"), aes(x = Ecoregion, y = Width_mm, fill = Coastal_Proximity)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha=0.7) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(3,24)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Size (Width mm)") +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Ecoregion") +
  ggtitle("(a) Pleistocene") +
=======








library("ggsignif")
library("ggpubr")

Fig4a <- ggplot(subset(Clean, Epoch=="Pleistocene"), aes(x = Ecoregion, y = Geometric_Mean, fill = Coastal_Proximity)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha=0.7) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(3,24)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Mass (Geometric Mean)") +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Ecoregion") +
  ggtitle("(a) Pleistocene") +
  geom_signif(comparisons=list("Ecoregion","Coastal_Proximity"), map_signif_level=TRUE) + 
  stat_compare_means(method = "t.test") +
>>>>>>> main
  theme_bw() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, family = "Tahoma", face = "bold"),
        text = element_text(size = 12, family = "Tahoma"),
        axis.title = element_text(face="bold"),
        axis.text.x=element_text(size = 11)) 
Fig4a
<<<<<<< HEAD
ggsave("Fig4a_Data-Clean-New_Ecoregion-Proximity-Width.png", dpi = 800)

Fig4b <- ggplot(subset(Clean_New, Epoch=="Holocene"), aes(x = Ecoregion, y = Width_mm, fill = Coastal_Proximity)) +
=======

  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(3,24)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Size (Width mm)") +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Ecoregion") +
  ggtitle("(b) Holocene") +

        text = element_text(size = 12, family = "Tahoma"),

        axis.text.x=element_text(size = 11))

  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(3,24)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Size (Width mm)") +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Ecoregion") +
  ggtitle("(c) Anthropocene") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, family = "Tahoma", face = "bold"),
        text = element_text(size = 12, family = "Tahoma"),
```
